op_000007.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α], Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α)

op_000007.statement_formalization_notes: Used fully qualified names for the namespace-local classes and fixpoint abbreviations from `Theory.lean`.

op_000007.proof_sketch: The closest reusable fact is `thm_godel_fixpoint_iff_reft_000002`, which converts `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` into the reft-only condition `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤`. I can also see from `gf_equiv_reft_top` and `reft_le_prov_reft` that a Gödel witness forces `⊠⊤ ≤ □(⊠⊤)`, so `⊠⊤` is a plausible Henkin candidate. The missing bridge is the reverse inequality `□(⊠⊤) ≤ ⊠⊤`, and nothing in the listed axioms seems to provide a way to turn a `□`-statement back into a `⊠`-statement. Symmetrically, from a Henkin witness `h ≐ □h` I do not see a route to derive `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` or to construct any `g` with `g ≐ ⊠g`. So I do not have a proof, but I also cannot certify a counterexample from the given axioms alone.
op_000007.counterexample_text: A concrete counterexample candidate is a finite preorder with three elements `0, a, b`, where `0` is bottom and `a,b` are distinct but top-equivalent (`a ≤ b` and `b ≤ a`). Let `⊠0 = a`, `⊠a = b`, `⊠b = b`, so `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` is plausible and hence the Gödel side may hold. Try to define `□` monotone so that `□b` is above `b` only enough to satisfy `reft_le_prov_reft`, but `□x` is never equivalent to `x`; this would separate Gödel from Henkin. I have not verified the APS axiom `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` for this structure, so this remains only a candidate refutation direction.

op_000007.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [Prov α] [Reft α] [APS α] [C5 α], Nonempty (HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ Nonempty (GödelFixpoint α)

op_000007.statement_formalization_notes: Used fully qualified names for the namespace-scoped classes and fixpoint abbreviations from Theory.lean.

op_000007.proof_sketch: Take the two-element chain `0 < 1`, with `⊥ = 0` and `⊤ = 1`, and let the provability operator be the identity: `□0 = 0`, `□1 = 1`. Let the refutability operator flip the chain: `⊠0 = 1` and `⊠1 = 0`; this is anti-monotone, satisfies `⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥`, and also satisfies `⊠x ≤ □⊠x` because `□` is the identity. The APS axiom `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` holds by case split on `y`: if `y = 0`, then `x ≤ □0 = 0`, so the conclusion `x ≤ ⊠⊤ = 0` is immediate, and if `y = 1`, then the premise `x ≤ ⊠1 = 0` already gives the same conclusion. In this model `HenkinFixpoint` is nonempty because every element satisfies `h ≐ □h`, but `GödelFixpoint` is empty because `⊠` swaps `0` and `1`, so no element is equivalent to its `⊠`-image.
op_000007.counterexample_text: Concrete model: the preorder is the two-element chain `0 < 1` with ordinary order, `⊤ = 1`, `⊥ = 0`, `□ = id`, and `⊠` given by Boolean negation (`⊠0 = 1`, `⊠1 = 0`). Then `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)` holds since `0 ≐ □0` and `1 ≐ □1`. But `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` fails, because equivalence in this partial order is equality and neither `0 = ⊠0 = 1` nor `1 = ⊠1 = 0`.

op_000012.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a precise Lean proposition and matches the notation and names from Theory.lean, so I preserved it verbatim up to spacing.

op_000012.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by instantiating it with a concrete three-element preorder `Three = {zero < one < two}`. Let `⊤ = two`, `⊥ = zero`, `□ = id`, and define `⊠` by `zero ↦ two`, `one ↦ two`, `two ↦ one`. The APS and C5 axioms are verified by finite case splits. In this model, `Consistent Three` holds and `¬ ((⊠ ⊤) ≤ ⊥)` holds because `⊠ ⊤ = one` and `one ≤ zero` is false. But there is no Gödel fixpoint: for each `g`, the conjunction `g ≤ ⊠ g ∧ ⊠ g ≤ g` reduces to `False` by inspection of the three cases. Applying the assumed universal equivalence to this model yields `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint Three)`, contradicting the case analysis.
op_000012.proof_text:
by
  intro h
  have hcons : ACR.Consistent Three := by
    simp [ACR.Consistent, ACR.Inconsistent, Three.le]
  have hnr : ¬ ((⊠ (⊤ : Three)) ≤ ⊥) := by
    simp [Three.le, Three.reft]
  rcases (h hcons).2 hnr with ⟨⟨g, hg⟩⟩
  cases g <;> simp [ACR.Equivalent, Three.le, Three.reft] at hg
op_000012.counterexample_text: Take `α = Three` with order `zero < one < two`, bottom `zero`, top `two`, provability operator `□ = id`, and refutability operator `⊠` given by `⊠ zero = two`, `⊠ one = two`, `⊠ two = one`. This satisfies `ACR`, `Prov`, `Reft`, `APS`, and `C5`. The theory is consistent, and `¬ ((⊠ ⊤) ≤ ⊥)` holds because `⊠ ⊤ = one` and `one ≤ zero` is false. But there is no Gödel fixpoint: `zero ≐ ⊠ zero` would require `two ≤ zero`, `one ≐ ⊠ one` would require `two ≤ one`, and `two ≐ ⊠ two` would require `two ≤ one`, all impossible. Hence the displayed equivalence fails in this model.

Repair by replacing the APS/C5 verification scripts with direct finite case proofs using `cases ... <;> cases ... <;> decide`, then derive `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint Three)` from the assumed universal equivalence and eliminate it by case-splitting on the alleged fixpoint `g`. In the three branches, one conjunct of `g ≐ ⊠ g` reduces to `False` by `simpa [Three.le, Three.reft]`.
op_000012.proof_text:
intro h
have hcons : ACR.Consistent Three := by
  change ¬ Three.le Three.two Three.zero
  decide
have hnr : ¬ ((⊠ (⊤ : Three)) ≤ ⊥) := by
  change ¬ Three.le (Three.reft Three.two) Three.zero
  decide
have hfix : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint Three) := (h (α := Three) hcons).2 hnr
rcases hfix with ⟨⟨g, hg⟩⟩
cases g
· have : False := by
    simpa [ACR.Equivalent, Three.le, Three.reft] using hg.2
  exact this.elim
· have : False := by
    simpa [ACR.Equivalent, Three.le, Three.reft] using hg.2
  exact this.elim
· have : False := by
    simpa [ACR.Equivalent, Three.le, Three.reft] using hg.1
  exact this.elim
op_000012.counterexample_text: Take `α = Three` with order `zero < one < two`, bottom `zero`, top `two`, provability operator `□ = id`, and refutability operator `⊠` given by `⊠ zero = two`, `⊠ one = two`, `⊠ two = one`. This structure satisfies `ACR`, `Prov`, `Reft`, `APS`, and `C5` by finite case analysis. It is consistent since `two ≤ zero` is false, and `¬ ((⊠ ⊤) ≤ ⊥)` holds because `⊠ ⊤ = one` and `one ≤ zero` is false. But there is no Gödel fixpoint: for `g = zero` or `g = one`, the backward inequality `⊠ g ≤ g` fails, and for `g = two`, the forward inequality `g ≤ ⊠ g` fails. But the Lean formalization is not yet defensible because the current model proof does not verify: the APS/C5 case proofs still fail, and the universal theorem cannot yet be specialized to the small model without a universe lift.

op_000013.source_statement: ∃ (α : Type*), ACR α ∧ ACR.Prov α ∧ ACR.Reft α ∧ ACR.APS α ∧ ACR.C5 α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
op_000013.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)
op_000013.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the conjunction of class assumptions by existentially quantifying the corresponding instances, since `ACR α`, `ACR.Prov α`, etc. are typeclass structures rather than propositions.

op_000013.proof_sketch: Use a lifted two-point chain with identity provability and a swapping refutability operator. Then Henkin fixpoints exist trivially, while a Gödel fixpoint would force either `tt ≤ ff` or `tt ≤ ff`, impossible by cases.
op_000013.prelude_code:
inductive Two where
  | ff
  | tt
deriving DecidableEq

instance : LE Two where
  le x y := match x, y with
    | .ff, _ => True
    | .tt, .tt => True
    | .tt, .ff => False

instance : Top Two where
  top := .tt

instance : Bot Two where
  bot := .ff

instance : Preorder Two where
  le := (· ≤ ·)
  le_refl := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> trivial
  le_trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    cases a <;> cases b <;> cases c <;> trivial

instance : ACR (ULift Two) where
  toTop := ULift.instTop
  toBot := ULift.instBot
  toPreorder := ULift.instPreorder

instance : ACR.Prov (ULift Two) where
  prov := id

instance : ACR.Reft (ULift Two) where
  reft x := match x.down with
    | .ff => ⟨.tt⟩
    | .tt => ⟨.ff⟩

instance : ACR.APS (ULift Two) where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    simpa using h
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases y using ULift.casesOn with
      | _ y =>
        cases x <;> cases y <;> trivial
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    trivial
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hxy hxr
    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases y using ULift.casesOn with
      | _ y =>
        cases x <;> cases y <;> trivial
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases x <;> trivial

instance : ACR.C5 (ULift Two) where
  le_top := by
    intro x
    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases x <;> trivial
op_000013.proof_text:
refine ⟨ULift Two, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, ?_⟩
constructor
· refine ⟨⟨⊥, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor <;> rfl
· intro h
  rcases h with ⟨⟨g, hg⟩⟩
  cases g using ULift.casesOn with
  | _ g =>
    cases g with
    | ff => exact hg.2
    | tt => exact hg.1

Hence `⟨⊠⊤, ...⟩` is an element of `ACR.GödelFixpoint α`, giving `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`, contradicting `hNo`. Therefore the existential statement is false.

  refine ⟨⟨⊠(⊤ : α), ?_⟩⟩
  simpa [ACR.Equivalent] using And.symm hEq

Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))`, one would need a general argument showing every APS-model with a Gödel fixpoint also has a Henkin fixpoint. The available verified bridge `thm_godel_implies_henkin_fixpoint_000014` requires the extra assumption `[ACR.C5 α]`, and its proof genuinely uses `le_top`. I did not find a theorem in the provided context eliminating the need for `C5`, and the one-point model only shows that some model has both kinds of fixpoints, which is insufficient to negate the existential.

That does not contradict the target statement, which asks for the existence of some possibly different APS-model with a Gödel fixpoint but no Henkin fixpoint. So this is not a valid counterexample to the existential.

op_000022.proof_sketch: The closest reusable theorem is `thm_inconsistent_godel_model_exists_000009`, but it only gives a Gödel-fixpoint witness under the extra hypothesis `ACR.C5 α`. In the same development, the known bridge from a Gödel fixpoint to a Henkin fixpoint also uses `C5`, so that witness cannot be turned into the target model with `¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`. I do not see any provided theorem that removes `C5` from either direction or directly constructs a model with Gödel but no Henkin fixpoint. Conceptually checking very small finite models also runs into the APS axioms: the easy choices that make a Gödel fixpoint exist tend either to force an immediate Henkin witness or to break `top_le_reft_bot` or `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft`. So I cannot currently prove or refute the existential from the supplied facts.

But this candidate already fails `top_le_reft_bot`, because with `Reft = id` that axiom becomes `⊤ ≤ ⊥`. Another nearby candidate is `Reft` constantly `⊤`, but then `reft_le_prov_reft` forces `□⊤ = ⊤`, making `⊤` an immediate Henkin fixpoint.

op_000018.source_statement: There exists `α` with `ACR α`, `ACR.Prov α`, `ACR.Reft α`, and `ACR.APS α` such that `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` holds but `¬(⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)`.
op_000018.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type*), ∃ (_ : ACR α), ∃ (_ : ACR.Prov α), ∃ (_ : ACR.Reft α), ∃ (_ : ACR.APS α), Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬(⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤)
op_000018.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the target as an existential over a type with the required ACR structure and modal operators, using repository notation in the conclusion.

op_000018.proof_sketch: Use a three-point witness `Tri` with preorder `x ≤ y :≡ triCode y ≤ triCode x`, but choose the ACR top/bot operations independently as `⊤ = two` and `⊥ = zero`. Let `□` be constantly `zero`, and let `⊠` send `zero ↦ one`, `one ↦ one`, `two ↦ zero`. Then `one ≐ ⊠ one`, so a Gödel fixpoint exists. Also `⊠⊤ = zero` and `⊠⊠⊤ = one`, and the second direction of their equivalence would require `zero ≤ one`, which is false in this preorder.
op_000018.prelude_code:
inductive Tri where
  | zero | one | two
  deriving DecidableEq

def triCode : Tri → Nat
  | .zero => 0
  | .one => 1
  | .two => 2

def triLE (x y : Tri) : Prop := triCode y ≤ triCode x

instance : DecidableRel triLE := fun _ _ => inferInstance

instance : ACR Tri where
  top := Tri.two
  bot := Tri.zero
  le := triLE
  le_refl := by
    intro x
    simpa [triLE] using (show triCode x ≤ triCode x from le_rfl)
  le_trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    simpa [triLE] using (Nat.le_trans hbc hab)

def triProv : Tri → Tri := fun _ => Tri.zero

instance : ACR.Prov Tri where
  prov := triProv

def triReft : Tri → Tri
  | .zero => .one
  | .one => .one
  | .two => .zero

instance : ACR.Reft Tri where
  reft := triReft

instance : ACR.APS Tri where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    simp [triLE, triCode, triProv]
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [triLE, triCode, triReft] at hxy ⊢
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    simp [triLE, triCode, triReft]
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hprov hreft
    simpa [triLE, triCode, triProv, triReft] using hprov
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    simp [triLE, triCode, triProv, triReft]
op_000018.proof_text:
refine ⟨Tri, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, ?_⟩
constructor
· refine ⟨⟨Tri.one, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor <;> simp [triLE, triCode, triReft]
· intro h
  simpa [triLE, triCode, triReft] using h.2

op_000017.source_statement: For every `α` with `ACR α` and `ACR.Reft α`, `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` is equivalent to `∃ g : α, g = ⊠(⊤ : α) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g`, and hence `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤` is exactly the condition that `⊠⊤` itself is a Gödel fixed point.
op_000017.formalized_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Reft α], (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ g : α, g = ⊠(⊤ : α) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g) ∧ ((⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)) ↔ ∃ g : α, g = ⊠(⊤ : α) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g)
op_000017.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the two sentence parts as a conjunction: existence of a Gödel fixpoint is equivalent to having witness `⊠⊤`, and the modal equation is equivalent to `⊠⊤` being such a witness.

op_000017.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by instantiating it with a concrete 3-point `ACR` whose preorder is equality, with `⊤ = zero`, `⊥ = one`, and `⊠` given by `zero ↦ one`, `one ↦ two`, `two ↦ two`. Then `two` is a Gödel fixed point, so the left side of the first equivalence holds. If the equivalence were true, there would be some `g = ⊠⊤ = one` with `g ≐ ⊠g`, but then `one ≐ two`, impossible in the equality preorder. Hence the first equivalence fails, so the whole conjunction is false.
op_000017.prelude_code:
inductive CounterexampleThree where
  | zero | one | two
deriving DecidableEq

def counterexampleReft : CounterexampleThree → CounterexampleThree
  | CounterexampleThree.zero => CounterexampleThree.one
  | CounterexampleThree.one => CounterexampleThree.two
  | CounterexampleThree.two => CounterexampleThree.two

instance : ACR CounterexampleThree where
  top := CounterexampleThree.zero
  bot := CounterexampleThree.one
  le x y := x = y
  le_refl := by
    intro x
    rfl
  le_trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    simpa [hab] using hbc

instance : ACR.Reft CounterexampleThree where
  reft := counterexampleReft
op_000017.proof_text:
intro h
have hiff := (h CounterexampleThree).1
have hgf : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint CounterexampleThree) := by
  refine ⟨⟨CounterexampleThree.two, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor <;> rfl
have hex : ∃ g : CounterexampleThree, g = ⊠(⊤ : CounterexampleThree) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g :=
  hiff.mp hgf
rcases hex with ⟨g, hg, hfix⟩
have hg' : g = CounterexampleThree.one := by
  simpa [counterexampleReft] using hg
subst g
have hone : CounterexampleThree.one = CounterexampleThree.two := by
  simpa [counterexampleReft] using hfix.1
cases hone

But `⊠⊤ = one`, and `one` is not a Gödel fixed point because `⊠one = two`, so `∃ g : α, g = ⊠(⊤ : α) ∧ g ≐ ⊠g` is false. Therefore the first claimed equivalence fails, and so the whole conjunction is false.

op_000026.proof_sketch: Use `Nat` with `⊤ = 1`, `⊥ = 0`, `□ = Nat.succ`, and constant `⊠ = 1`. Then the APS axioms are immediate, the system is consistent since `¬ (1 ≤ 0)`, `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` because both are `1`, and a Henkin fixpoint would force `n.succ ≤ n`, impossible in `Nat`.

instance natACR : ACR Nat where
  top := 1
  bot := 0
  toPreorder := inferInstance

instance natProv : ACR.Prov Nat where
  prov := Nat.succ

instance natReft : ACR.Reft Nat where
  reft _ := 1

instance natAPS : ACR.APS Nat where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    exact Nat.succ_le_succ h
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    simpa [natReft] using (show (1 : Nat) ≤ 1 by exact le_rfl)
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    simpa [natACR, natReft] using (show (1 : Nat) ≤ 1 by exact le_rfl)
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hxy hxr
    simpa [natACR, natReft] using hxr
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    norm_num [natProv, natReft]

· show ¬ ((1 : Nat) ≤ 0)
  decide
constructor
· constructor <;> decide
· intro h
  rcases h with ⟨⟨n, hn⟩⟩
  exact Nat.not_succ_le_self n (by simpa [natProv, ACR.Equivalent] using hn.2)

op_000025.formalized_statement: ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)

op_000025.proof_sketch: The provided reusable theorems do not reach this existential. `thm_inconsistent_godel_model_exists_000009` gives a Gödel fixpoint only in an inconsistent model, while `thm_henkin_without_godel_model_000013` goes in the opposite direction and produces a Henkin fixpoint with no Gödel fixpoint, so neither can witness `ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`. From a Gödel fixpoint `g`, the axioms give the forward bridge `g.1 ≤ □g.1` using `g.2.1` together with `ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf`, but I still do not see a way either to force `□g.1 ≤ g.1` and obtain a Henkin fixpoint or to derive that Henkin fixpoints are impossible. Consistency only yields `ACR.irrefutable_reft_top`, which constrains `⊠⊤` but does not connect directly to `ACR.HenkinFixpoint`. So I cannot currently prove or refute the target from the stated axioms and derived facts.
op_000025.counterexample_text: A plausible countermodel candidate for the existential being false is a preorder with three points `⊥ < m < ⊤`, with `⊠x := m` for all `x` and `□x := ⊤` for all `x`. Then `m ≐ ⊠m`, so a Gödel fixpoint exists, while a Henkin fixpoint would require some `h` with `⊤ = □h ≤ h`, forcing `h = ⊤`, and one can try to arrange that `⊤ ≤ □⊤` but not `□⊤ ≤ ⊤` as an actual equivalence witness. However, I have not checked that this candidate really satisfies all APS axioms, especially `top_le_reft_bot` and `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft`, so it remains only a speculative direction.

For the Gödel fixpoint, take `g := ⊤`; again subsingleton collapse gives `⊤ = ⊠⊤` and `⊠⊤ = ⊤`, so both inequalities in `g ≐ ⊠g` are immediate by `le_rfl` after rewriting.

constructor
· intro hC
  apply hC
  change ((⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥)
  simpa [Subsingleton.elim (⊤ : α) (⊥ : α)] using (le_rfl : (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊤)
· refine ⟨⟨⊤, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · simpa [Subsingleton.elim (⊤ : α) (⊠ (⊤ : α))] using (le_rfl : (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊤)
  · simpa [Subsingleton.elim (⊠ (⊤ : α)) (⊤ : α)] using (le_rfl : (⊠ (⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊠ (⊤ : α))

op_000024.proof_sketch: Use the four-point preorder suggested in the attempt: elements `b, t, a, p`, with only nontrivial order relations `t ≤ a` and `p ≤ a` besides reflexivity. Let `□x := a` for all `x`, and define `⊠b = t`, `⊠t = a`, `⊠a = p`, `⊠p = p`. Then the APS bridge axiom is immediate from the premise `x ≤ □y = a`, consistency is `¬ (t ≤ b)`, `p` is a Gödel fixpoint since `⊠p = p`, and `⊠⊠⊤ = p` while `⊠⊤ = a`, so equivalence would force the false relation `a ≤ p`.

inductive Op000024Model where
  | b | t | a | p
deriving DecidableEq

def Op000024Model.le : Op000024Model → Op000024Model → Prop
  | .b, .b => True
  | .t, .t => True
  | .a, .a => True
  | .p, .p => True
  | .t, .a => True
  | .p, .a => True
  | _, _ => False

def Op000024Model.prov : Op000024Model → Op000024Model
  | _ => .a

def Op000024Model.reft : Op000024Model → Op000024Model
  | .b => .t
  | .t => .a
  | .a => .p
  | .p => .p

instance : ACR Op000024Model where
  top := Op000024Model.t
  bot := Op000024Model.b
  le := Op000024Model.le
  le_refl := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> simp [Op000024Model.le]
  le_trans := by
    intro x y z hxy hyz
    cases x <;> cases y <;> cases z <;> simp [Op000024Model.le] at hxy hyz ⊢

instance : ACR.Prov Op000024Model where
  prov := Op000024Model.prov

instance : ACR.Reft Op000024Model where
  reft := Op000024Model.reft

instance : ACR.APS Op000024Model where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    simp [Op000024Model.prov, Op000024Model.le]
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y hxy
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [Op000024Model.le, Op000024Model.reft] at hxy ⊢
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    simp [Op000024Model.reft, Op000024Model.le]
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hxy hy
    simpa [Op000024Model.prov, Op000024Model.reft] using hxy
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> simp [Op000024Model.reft, Op000024Model.prov, Op000024Model.le]

op_000029.source_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [Finite α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000029.formalized_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [Finite α] [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000029.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a well-formed Lean proposition using the repository's notation, so I preserved it verbatim.

op_000029.proof_sketch: Let `g := ⊠⊤`. The hypothesis `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` gives `g ≤ ⊠g`, and `⊠g ≤ □g` follows from `reft_le_prov_reft`, so `g ≤ □g`. With `f := □`, monotonicity of `□` implies the orbit `n ↦ f^[n] g` is monotone. Since `α` is finite but this orbit is indexed by `ℕ`, two iterates coincide: `f^[m] g = f^[n] g` with `m ≠ n`. Taking the smaller index, monotonicity of the orbit gives `f^[m] g ≤ f^[m+1] g ≤ f^[n] g = f^[m] g`, hence `f^[m] g ≐ □(f^[m] g)`, yielding a Henkin fixpoint.
op_000029.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ _ htop
let f : α → α := fun x => □x
let g : α := ⊠ (⊤ : α)
have hmono : Monotone f := by
  intro x y hxy
  exact ACR.prov_mono hxy
have hg : g ≤ f g := by
  calc
    g ≤ ⊠g := by
      simpa [g] using htop.2
    _ ≤ f g := by
      simpa [f] using (ACR.reft_le_prov_reft (x := g))
have horbit : Monotone fun n : ℕ => f^[n] g :=
  Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map hmono hg
obtain ⟨m, n, heq, hne⟩ : ∃ m n, f^[m] g = f^[n] g ∧ m ≠ n := by
  simpa [Function.Injective] using not_injective_infinite_finite (fun k : ℕ => f^[k] g)
rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hne with hmn | hnm
· refine ⟨⟨f^[m] g, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
  · have hle : f^[m + 1] g ≤ f^[n] g := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)
    simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply, heq] using hle
· refine ⟨⟨f^[n] g, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply] using horbit (Nat.le_succ n)
  · have hle : f^[n + 1] g ≤ f^[m] g := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hnm)
    simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply, heq] using hle

op_000029.proof_sketch: The previous proof idea is salvageable. Let `f x := □x` and start from `g := ⊠⊤`. We do not need the hypothesis `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` for the inflation step: `g ≤ f g` follows directly from `reft_le_prov_reft` at `x = ⊤`. Since `f` is monotone and `α` is finite, the orbit `n ↦ f^[n] g` is a monotone sequence in a finite type, so two distinct iterates coincide. Taking the smaller index gives `f^[m] g ≤ f^[m+1] g ≤ f^[n] g = f^[m] g`, hence `f^[m] g ≐ □(f^[m] g)`, producing a Henkin fixpoint. The repair is to use the correct inflation lemma for `g`, and to rewrite iterate steps with `Function.iterate_succ_apply'` instead of the mismatched `iterate_succ_apply`.

· refine ⟨⟨f^[m] g, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
  · have hle : f^[m + 1] g ≤ f^[n] g := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)

op_000030.source_statement: If β carries a preorder with distinguished elements ⊤ and ⊥, an order-preserving operator □ with ∀ x, ¬ (□x ≤ x), and a point c with ⊤ ≤ c and ¬ (⊤ ≤ ⊥), then there is an APS expansion on β with ⊠x = c for all x such that ACR.Consistent β ∧ (⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint β)
op_000030.formalized_statement: ∀ (β : Type*) [ACR β] [ACR.Prov β], (∀ {x y : β}, x ≤ y → □x ≤ □y) → (∀ x : β, ¬ (□x ≤ x)) → ∀ c : β, ⊤ ≤ c → ¬ ((⊤ : β) ≤ ⊥) → ∃ (_ : ACR.Reft β) (_ : ACR.APS β), (∀ x : β, ⊠x = c) ∧ ACR.Consistent β ∧ (⊠⊠(⊤ : β) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : β)) ∧ ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint β)
op_000030.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the requested expansion theorem by quantifying over a base `ACR` type with a `Prov` operator, assuming monotonicity and absence of pre-fixpoints for `□`, then asserting existence of a `Reft`/`APS` expansion with constant `⊠ = c`.

op_000030.proof_sketch: The proposed ex-falso proof uses `le_top`, but `ACR` only extends `Top` and `Preorder`; the theorem `x ≤ ⊤` is not available without the stronger `ACR.C5` assumption. In fact the statement is false: take a two-element type with equality as the preorder and let `□` swap the two points. Then `□` is monotone and has no pre-fixpoints, but for `c = ⊤` any constant reflection operator `⊠x = c` would force `c ≤ □c` from the APS axiom `reft_le_prov_reft`, which fails.
op_000030.counterexample_text:
Counterexample model: let `β = Bool`, with preorder `x ≤ y :↔ x = y` (the discrete/equality preorder). Set `(⊤ : β) = true` and `(⊥ : β) = false`. Define provability by swapping the two elements: `□true = false` and `□false = true`.

Then the premises hold:
- Monotonicity is immediate, because `x ≤ y` means `x = y`, so `□x = □y`, hence `□x ≤ □y`.
- `∀ x, ¬ (□x ≤ x)` holds because `□x ≤ x` is just `□x = x`, but the swap map has no fixed points.
- Take `c = true`. Then `⊤ ≤ c` holds since `true = true`.
- `¬ ((⊤ : β) ≤ ⊥)` holds since `true ≠ false`.

But the conclusion fails. Suppose there were instances `ACR.Reft β` and `ACR.APS β` with `∀ x, ⊠x = c`, so in particular `⊠x = true` for all `x`. The APS axiom `reft_le_prov_reft` says `⊠x ≤ □⊠x` for every `x`. Under the equality preorder this means `⊠x = □⊠x`. Substituting `⊠x = true`, we get `true = □true = false`, impossible. So no such `Reft`/`APS` expansion exists.
op_000030.lean_error_excerpt: Lean verification succeeded but proof still contains sorry

op_000030.proof_sketch: Use the existing type `CounterexampleThree` from `Derived.lean`, where the `ACR` order is equality, `⊤ = zero`, and `⊥ = one`. Add a cyclic provability operator `zero ↦ one ↦ two ↦ zero`. Monotonicity is trivial because the order is equality, and `□x ≤ x` is impossible for every `x` because the cycle has no fixed point. Apply the claimed universal statement at `β = CounterexampleThree` and `c = zero`. If a constant reflection operator with `⊠x = zero` existed together with an `APS` structure, the axiom `reft_le_prov_reft` at `x = zero` would give `zero ≤ □zero`, i.e. `zero = one`, contradiction.
op_000030.prelude_code:
instance : ACR.Prov CounterexampleThree where
  prov x := match x with
    | CounterexampleThree.zero => CounterexampleThree.one
    | CounterexampleThree.one => CounterexampleThree.two
    | CounterexampleThree.two => CounterexampleThree.zero
op_000030.proof_text:
intro h
have hmono : ∀ {x y : CounterexampleThree}, x ≤ y → □x ≤ □y := by
  intro x y hxy
  cases hxy
  rfl
have hnfix : ∀ x : CounterexampleThree, ¬ (□x ≤ x) := by
  intro x hle
  cases x <;> cases hle
have hex := @h CounterexampleThree inferInstance inferInstance hmono hnfix CounterexampleThree.zero rfl (by intro hbot; cases hbot)
rcases hex with ⟨_hreft, _haps, hconst, _hcons, _hidem, _hnoHenkin⟩

change CounterexampleThree.zero = CounterexampleThree.one at hraw
cases hraw
op_000030.counterexample_text:
Take `β = CounterexampleThree`, already available in the theory development. Its `ACR` structure uses equality as the preorder, with `⊤ = zero` and `⊥ = one`. Define provability by the 3-cycle `□zero = one`, `□one = two`, `□two = zero`.

Then the assumptions of the target statement hold:
- Monotonicity of `□` is immediate because `x ≤ y` means `x = y`.
- For every `x`, `¬ (□x ≤ x)` holds because `□x ≤ x` again means `□x = x`, but the 3-cycle has no fixed point.
- Let `c = zero`. Then `⊤ ≤ c` is `zero = zero`, true.
- Also `¬ ((⊤ : β) ≤ ⊥)` is `¬ (zero = one)`, true.

Now suppose the conclusion held, so there were `ACR.Reft β` and `ACR.APS β` with `∀ x, ⊠x = c = zero`. The APS axiom `reft_le_prov_reft` applied to `x = zero` gives `⊠zero ≤ □(⊠zero)`. Replacing `⊠zero` by `zero`, this becomes `zero ≤ □zero`, hence `zero = one`, contradiction. Therefore the universal statement is false.

op_000032.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ x : α, x ≤ □x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((fun z => □z)^[n]) x = y}) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000032.statement_formalization_notes: Replaced the unavailable `Function.iterate` identifier with Lean's standard function-iteration notation `f^[n]`, preserving the original meaning of the orbit set.

op_000032.proof_sketch: Follow the already-verified finite-orbit argument from `thm_finite_topbox_henkin_exists_000029`, but with the given inflationary seed `x`. Let `f z := □z`. Then `f` is monotone by `ACR.prov_mono`, and from `x ≤ □x` the iterate orbit `n ↦ f^[n] x` is monotone via `Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map`. The forward orbit set is finite, so the map from `ℕ` into the orbit subtype is not injective; hence some two iterates coincide, say `f^[m] x = f^[n] x` with `m ≠ n`. If `m < n`, monotonicity gives `f^[m] x ≤ f^[m+1] x ≤ f^[n] x`, and the equality of endpoints yields `f^[m+1] x ≤ f^[m] x`. Thus `f^[m] x ≐ □(f^[m] x)`. The `n < m` case is symmetric.
op_000032.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ h
rcases h with ⟨x, hx, hfin⟩
let f : α → α := fun z => □z
have hmono : Monotone f := by
  intro a b hab
  exact ACR.prov_mono hab
have horbit : Monotone fun n : ℕ => f^[n] x :=
  Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map hmono (by simpa [f] using hx)
let s : Set α := {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, f^[n] x = y}
have hsfin : s.Finite := by
  simpa [s] using hfin
classical
letI := hsfin.fintype
let g : ℕ → s := fun k => ⟨f^[k] x, by exact ⟨k, rfl⟩⟩
obtain ⟨m, n, heq, hne⟩ : ∃ m n, g m = g n ∧ m ≠ n := by
  simpa [g, Function.Injective, and_comm] using
    not_injective_infinite_finite g
have heq' : f^[m] x = f^[n] x := by
  simpa [g] using congrArg Subtype.val heq
rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hne with hmn | hnm
· refine ⟨⟨f^[m] x, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
  · have hle : f^[m + 1] x ≤ f^[n] x := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)
    simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply', heq'] using hle
· refine ⟨⟨f^[n] x, ?_⟩⟩
  constructor
  · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ n)
  · have hle : f^[n + 1] x ≤ f^[m] x := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hnm)
    simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply', heq'] using hle

op_000038.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, Set.Infinite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, ((fun z => □z)^[n]) g.1 = y}

op_000038.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a precise Lean proposition; I preserved its quantifier order and the repository's box notation.

op_000038.proof_sketch: Assume the forward box-orbit of a Gödel fixpoint `g` is not infinite. From `g.2.1 : g.1 ≤ ⊠g.1` and `ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf`, we get `g.1 ≤ □g.1`, so `g.1` is a pre-fixed point of `□`. The verified theorem `thm_finite_box_orbit_henkin_000032` then turns finiteness of that orbit into `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`, contradicting the hypothesis. Hence the orbit set is infinite.

intro α _ _ _ _ _ _ hno g
by_contra hfinite
have hgbox : g.1 ≤ □g.1 := by
  exact le_trans g.2.1 (ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf (g := g))
exact hno <| AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_finite_box_orbit_henkin_000032 (α := α) ⟨g.1, hgbox, Set.not_infinite.mp hfinite⟩

op_000039.statement_formalization_notes: The statement is already precise in repository notation, so I kept it verbatim with no prelude.

op_000039.proof_sketch: Use the existing concrete separator model from `thm_consistent_topbox_no_henkin_000026`. In that model we already have consistency, `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤`, and `¬ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`. The only extra hypothesis needed to contradict the target implication is `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`, which follows from the already verified theorem `thm_reft_top_equiv_no_godel_000008_is_false`: any APS model with `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` cannot lack a Gödel fixpoint. Applying the assumed universal implication to this model yields a Henkin fixpoint, contradicting the separator theorem.

by
  intro h
  rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_consistent_topbox_no_henkin_000026 with
    ⟨α, hACR, hProv, hReft, hAPS, hNoHenkin⟩
  letI := hACR
  letI := hProv
  letI := hReft
  letI := hAPS
  have hEq : (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤) := hNoHenkin.2.1
  have hGodel : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := by
    by_contra hNoGodel
    exact AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_reft_top_equiv_no_godel_000008_is_false
      ⟨α, hACR, hProv, hReft, hAPS, hEq, hNoGodel⟩
  exact hNoHenkin.2.2 (h (α := α) hNoHenkin.1 hGodel hEq)

It is an `APS` structure with `ACR.Consistent α`, `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤`, and no Henkin fixpoint. The theorem `thm_reft_top_equiv_no_godel_000008_is_false` upgrades the same equality hypothesis to `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`. Therefore this single model satisfies all hypotheses of the target implication while falsifying its conclusion, so the universal statement is false.

op_000042.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((fun z => □z)^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((fun z => □z)^[n]) g.1) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000042.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (∃ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) g.1 ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) g.1) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
op_000042.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the iterate function by annotating the binder as `z : α` so Lean can resolve the `ACR.Prov α` instance without metavariables.

op_000042.proof_sketch: Take `f z := □z`. For the witness `g` from the repeated-iterate hypothesis, first show `g.1 ≤ □g.1` using `g.2.1` and `ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf`. Then `f` is monotone by `ACR.prov_mono`, so the orbit `n ↦ f^[n] g.1` is monotone via `Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map`. From `m < n` and `f^[m] g.1 ≐ f^[n] g.1`, monotonicity gives `f^[m+1] g.1 ≤ f^[n] g.1`, and the second half of the equivalence gives `f^[n] g.1 ≤ f^[m] g.1`. Thus `f^[m] g.1 ≐ f^[m+1] g.1`, yielding a Henkin fixpoint.
op_000042.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ hrep
rcases hrep with ⟨g, m, n, hmn, heq⟩
let f : α → α := fun z => □z
have hmono : Monotone f := by
  intro a b hab
  exact ACR.prov_mono hab
have hg : g.1 ≤ f g.1 := by
  show g.1 ≤ □g.1
  exact le_trans g.2.1 (ACR.reft_gf_le_box_gf (g := g))
have horbit : Monotone fun k : ℕ => f^[k] g.1 :=
  Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map hmono (by simpa [f] using hg)
refine ⟨⟨f^[m] g.1, ?_⟩⟩
constructor
· simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
· have hle : f^[m + 1] g.1 ≤ f^[n] g.1 := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)
  simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using le_trans hle heq.2

op_000044.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] (c : α), (Monotone (fun x : α => □x)) → ((∃ hR : ACR.Reft α, ∃ hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR, ∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α _ hR x = c) ↔ ((⊤ : α) ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c))
op_000044.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] (c : α), Monotone (fun x : α => □x) → ((∃ hR : ACR.Reft α, ∃ hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR, ∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α _ hR x = c) ↔ ((⊤ : α) ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c))
op_000044.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition; I preserved its explicit witness structure for `Reft` and `APS` and only normalized formatting.

op_000044.proof_sketch: Split the iff. From a constant `Reft`, use `top_le_reft_bot` and `reft_le_prov_reft` rewritten through the constant-value hypothesis. Conversely, from `(⊤ ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c)` and monotonicity of `□`, build the constant `Reft` instance `fun _ => c` and verify each `APS` field directly.
op_000044.proof_text:
intro α _ _ c hmono
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨hR, hA, hconst⟩
    letI : ACR.Reft α := hR
    letI : ACR.APS α := hA
    constructor
    · simpa [hconst ⊥] using (ACR.top_le_reft_bot (α := α))
    · simpa [hconst c] using (ACR.reft_le_prov_reft (α := α) (x := c))
  · rintro ⟨htop, hbox⟩
    let hR : ACR.Reft α := { reft := fun _ => c }
    have hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR :=
      { prov_mono := by
          intro x y hxy
          exact hmono hxy
        reft_anti_mono := by
          intro x y hxy
          exact le_rfl
        top_le_reft_bot := by
          simpa using htop
        le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
          intro x y hxy hxr
          simpa using hxr
        reft_le_prov_reft := by
          intro x
          simpa using hbox }
    exact ⟨hR, hA, by intro x; rfl⟩

op_000035.source_statement: In equality-preorder `ACR` models with monotone `□`, determine whether the existence of a constant-reflection `APS` is equivalent to the existence of some `c` with `c = □c`.
op_000035.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α], (∀ {x y : α}, x ≤ y ↔ x = y) → Monotone (fun x : α => □x) → ((∃ c : α, ∃ hR : ACR.Reft α, ∃ hA : @ACR.APS α _ _ hR, ∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α _ hR x = c) ↔ ∃ c : α, c = □c)
op_000035.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized equality-preorder as x ≤ y iff x = y, and encoded constant-reflection APS as a constant choice of reft witnessing APS.

op_000035.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by instantiating it with a two-point equality-order model. Let `α = ULift Op000035Bool`, with order given by equality on the underlying two-element type, `⊤ = tt`, `⊥ = ff`, and `□x = ff` constantly. Then the equality-order hypothesis holds, and monotonicity of `□` is trivial. The right-hand side of the claimed equivalence holds with witness `ff`, since `ff = □ff`. If the left-hand side held, there would be a constant reflection operator and an `APS` structure. Applying the verified theorem `thm_constant_reft_iff_bounds_000044` to that constant witness gives both `⊤ ≤ c` and `c ≤ □c`. In this model, the first forces `c = tt`, while the second forces `c = ff`, contradiction.
op_000035.prelude_code:
inductive Op000035Bool where
  | ff | tt
  deriving DecidableEq

instance : ACR (ULift Op000035Bool) where
  top := ⟨.tt⟩
  bot := ⟨.ff⟩
  le x y := x.down = y.down
  le_refl := by
    intro x
    rfl
  le_trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    simpa [hab] using hbc

instance : ACR.Prov (ULift Op000035Bool) where
  prov _ := ⟨.ff⟩
op_000035.proof_text:
intro h
have hEq : ∀ {x y : ULift Op000035Bool}, x ≤ y ↔ x = y := by
  intro x y
  constructor
  · intro hxy
    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases y using ULift.casesOn with
      | _ y =>
        cases x <;> cases y <;> cases hxy <;> rfl
  · intro hxy
    cases hxy
    rfl
have hMono : Monotone (fun x : ULift Op000035Bool => □x) := by
  intro x y hxy
  rfl
have hiff := h (α := ULift Op000035Bool) hEq hMono
have hleft : ∃ c : ULift Op000035Bool, ∃ hR : ACR.Reft (ULift Op000035Bool), ∃ hA : @ACR.APS (ULift Op000035Bool) _ _ hR, ∀ x : ULift Op000035Bool, @ACR.Reft.reft (ULift Op000035Bool) _ hR x = c :=
  hiff.2 ⟨⟨Op000035Bool.ff⟩, rfl⟩
rcases hleft with ⟨c, hR, hA, hconst⟩
letI : ACR.Reft (ULift Op000035Bool) := hR
letI : ACR.APS (ULift Op000035Bool) := hA
have hbounds := (thm_constant_reft_iff_bounds_000044 (α := ULift Op000035Bool) (c := c) hMono).1 ⟨hR, hA, hconst⟩
cases c using ULift.casesOn with
| _ c =>
    cases c with
    | ff =>
        cases hbounds.1
    | tt =>
        cases hbounds.2
op_000035.counterexample_text: Take `α = ULift Op000035Bool` where `Op000035Bool = {ff, tt}`. Define the preorder by equality (`x ≤ y :↔ x = y`), let `⊤ = tt`, `⊥ = ff`, and define `□x = ff` for all `x`. Then `□` is monotone, and the RHS of the target equivalence holds because `ff = □ff`. If a constant reflection operator existed together with an `APS` structure, the verified constant-reflection criterion `thm_constant_reft_iff_bounds_000044` would force its constant value `c` to satisfy both `⊤ ≤ c` and `c ≤ □c`, hence `c = tt` and `c = ff` simultaneously, impossible.

op_000050.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] (c : α), let hR : ACR.Reft α := { reft := fun _ : α => c }; (@ACR.APS α _ _ hR ↔ (Monotone (fun x : α => □x) ∧ (⊤ : α) ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c))

op_000050.statement_formalization_notes: Interpreted `@ACR.APS ...` propositionally as existence of an APS instance, using `Nonempty` because `APS` is a class/structure rather than a proposition.

op_000050.proof_sketch: Introduce the constant `Reft` instance as a local `let`/`letI`, reduce the statement to that explicit instance, then extract the three needed conditions from an `APS` witness and conversely build an `APS` structure directly. The constant-reflection fields collapse by `simpa [hR]`.

intro α _ _ c
let hR : ACR.Reft α := { reft := fun _ : α => c }
letI : ACR.Reft α := hR
change Nonempty (@ACR.APS α _ _ hR) ↔
    (Monotone (fun x : α => □x) ∧ (⊤ : α) ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c)
constructor
· rintro ⟨hA⟩
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · intro x y hxy
    exact hA.prov_mono hxy
  · simpa [hR] using hA.top_le_reft_bot
  · simpa [hR] using (hA.reft_le_prov_reft (x := ⊥))
· rintro ⟨hm, htop, hpc⟩
  refine ⟨{
    prov_mono := ?_,
    reft_anti_mono := ?_,
    top_le_reft_bot := ?_,
    le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := ?_,
    reft_le_prov_reft := ?_
  }⟩
  · intro x y hxy
    exact hm hxy
  · intro x y hxy
    simpa [hR]
  · simpa [hR] using htop
  · intro x y hxy hxr
    simpa [hR] using hxr
  · intro x
    simpa [hR] using hpc

op_000051.proof_sketch: The intended route is to use `thm_constant_reft_iff_bounds_000044` to translate between constant-`Reft` APS existence and the bounds `(⊤ ≤ c ∧ c ≤ □c)`, and extract/provide monotonicity via `APS.prov_mono`. However, the reverse direction requires transporting an `APS` instance along an equality of `Reft` instances from an existential witness `hR` with `∀ x, hR.reft x = c` to the literal structure `{ reft := fun _ => c }`. Without the exact verified statement and usable extensionality/rewrite behavior for `ACR.Reft`/`APS` in this environment, I cannot defend a Lean script with high confidence.

op_000051.proof_text:
intro α _ _
constructor
· rintro ⟨c, hA⟩

op_000037.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y}) → ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z => □z)^[k]) h
op_000037.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (∃ x : α, x ≤ boxk k x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (boxk k)^[n] x = y}) → ∃ h : α, h ≐ boxk k h
op_000037.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced a small local abbreviation to keep the repository's iterate notation while fixing the earlier metavariable issue by explicitly typing the box-map domain.

op_000037.proof_sketch: Repeat the finite-orbit argument for the monotone map `boxk k`. Monotonicity comes from monotonicity of `□` and `Monotone.iterate`. From `x ≤ boxk k x`, the orbit `n ↦ (boxk k)^[n] x` is monotone. Finite orbit gives a repeated pair of orbit points, and monotonicity between the two indices yields equivalence of one point with its successor, giving a `boxk k`-fixed point up to `≐`.
op_000037.prelude_code: abbrev boxk {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] (k : ℕ) : α → α := (fun z : α => □z)^[k]
op_000037.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ k hk h
  rcases h with ⟨x, hx, hfin⟩
  let f : α → α := boxk k
  have hmonoBox : Monotone (fun z : α => □z) := by
    intro a b hab
    exact ACR.prov_mono hab
  have hmono : Monotone f := by
    simpa [f, boxk] using hmonoBox.iterate k
  have horbit : Monotone fun n : ℕ => f^[n] x :=
    Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map hmono (by simpa [f] using hx)
  let s : Set α := {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, f^[n] x = y}
  have hsfin : s.Finite := by
    simpa [s] using hfin
  classical
  letI := hsfin.fintype
  let g : ℕ → s := fun n => ⟨f^[n] x, ⟨n, rfl⟩⟩
  obtain ⟨m, n, heq, hne⟩ : ∃ m n, g m = g n ∧ m ≠ n := by
    simpa [g, Function.Injective, and_comm] using not_injective_infinite_finite g
  have hmn_eq : f^[m] x = f^[n] x := by
    simpa [g] using congrArg Subtype.val heq
  rcases lt_or_gt_of_ne hne with hmn | hnm
  · refine ⟨f^[m] x, ?_⟩
    constructor
    · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
    · have hle : f^[m + 1] x ≤ f^[n] x := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)
      simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply', hmn_eq] using hle
  · refine ⟨f^[n] x, ?_⟩
    constructor
    · simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ n)
    · have hle : f^[n + 1] x ≤ f^[m] x := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hnm)
      simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply', hmn_eq] using hle

op_000046.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {x : α} {m n : ℕ}, x ≤ □x → m < n → ((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) x → ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
op_000046.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] {x : α} {m n : ℕ}, x ≤ □x → m < n → ((fun z : α => □z)^[m]) x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[n]) x → ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
op_000046.statement_formalization_notes: The natural-language target already matches repository notation and parses directly with `import Mathlib`; no auxiliary prelude is needed.

op_000046.proof_sketch: Let `f := fun z => □z` and `h := (f^[m]) x`. Monotonicity of `□` implies every iterate `f^[k]` is monotone, hence from `x ≤ □x` the orbit is inflationary. Writing `n = m + d + 1`, the repetition hypothesis gives `(f^[d+1]) h ≤ h`. Since `h ≤ □h`, the point `□h` is also inflationary, so `□h ≤ (f^[d+1]) h`. Therefore `□h ≤ h`, and together with `h ≤ □h` we obtain `h ≐ □h`.
op_000046.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ x m n hx hmn hEq
let f : α → α := fun z : α => □z
have hmono_iter : ∀ {y z : α}, y ≤ z → ∀ k : ℕ, (f^[k]) y ≤ (f^[k]) z := by
  intro y z hyz k
  induction k with
  | zero =>
      simpa using hyz
  | succ k ih =>
      simpa [Function.iterate_succ_apply, f] using prov_mono ih
have hiterate_infl : ∀ {y : α}, y ≤ f y → ∀ k : ℕ, y ≤ (f^[k]) y := by
  intro y hy k
  induction k with
  | zero =>
      rfl
  | succ k ih =>
      exact le_trans ih (by
        have : (f^[k]) y ≤ (f^[k]) (f y) := hmono_iter hy k
        simpa [Function.iterate_succ_apply', f] using this)
obtain ⟨d, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hmn
let h : α := (f^[m]) x
refine ⟨h, ?_⟩
have hm : h ≤ f h := by
  have : (f^[m]) x ≤ (f^[m]) (f x) := hmono_iter hx m
  simpa [h, Function.iterate_succ_apply', f] using this
have hbox_le_iter : f h ≤ (f^[d + 1]) h := by
  have hf_infl : f h ≤ f (f h) := prov_mono hm
  have : f h ≤ (f^[d]) (f h) := hiterate_infl hf_infl d
  simpa [Function.iterate_succ_apply', f] using this
have hbox_le_h : f h ≤ h := by
  have : f h ≤ (f^[m + d + 1]) x := by
    simpa [h, Function.iterate_add_apply, f] using hbox_le_iter
  exact le_trans this hEq.2
constructor
· simpa [f] using hm
· simpa [f] using hbox_le_h

op_000046.proof_sketch: Let `f := fun z => □z`. Since `□` is monotone by `ACR.prov_mono`, the orbit `n ↦ (f^[n]) x` is monotone whenever `x ≤ □x`, via `Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map`. Take `h := (f^[m]) x`. Then monotonicity gives `h ≤ □h`. Also `m < n` implies `(f^[m+1]) x ≤ (f^[n]) x`; composing this with the backward direction of the given equivalence `((f^[m]) x ≐ (f^[n]) x)` yields `□h ≤ h`. Hence `h ≐ □h`.
op_000046.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ x m n hx hmn hEq
let f : α → α := fun z : α => □z
have hmono : Monotone f := by
  intro a b hab
  exact ACR.prov_mono hab
have horbit : Monotone (fun t : ℕ => f^[t] x) :=
  Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map hmono (by simpa [f] using hx)
refine ⟨f^[m] x, ?_⟩
constructor
· simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
· have hle : f^[m + 1] x ≤ f^[n] x := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)
  have hback : f^[n] x ≤ f^[m] x := by
    simpa [f] using hEq.2
  exact le_trans (by simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using hle) hback

op_000047.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ) {x : α} {m n : ℕ}, 0 < k → x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x → m < n → ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x) → ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h
op_000047.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ) {x : α} {m n : ℕ}, 0 < k → x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x → m < n → ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x) → ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h
op_000047.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized directly from the given statement using iterate notation for the k-step box map and repository notation `≐` and `□`.

op_000047.proof_sketch: Let f := ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]). The assumption x ≤ f x and monotonicity of □ imply the orbit t ↦ f^[t] x is monotone. Taking h := f^[m] x, we get h ≤ f h from monotonicity. Since m < n, also f h = f^[m+1] x ≤ f^[n] x, and the given equivalence f^[m] x ≐ f^[n] x gives f^[n] x ≤ f^[m] x = h. Hence f h ≤ h, so h ≐ f h.
op_000047.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ k x m n _hk hx hmn hrepeat
let f : α → α := ((fun z : α => □z)^[k])
have hmonoBox : Monotone (fun z : α => □z) := by
  intro a b hab
  exact ACR.prov_mono hab
have hmono : Monotone f := by
  simpa [f] using hmonoBox.iterate k
have horbit : Monotone (fun t : ℕ => f^[t] x) :=
  Monotone.monotone_iterate_of_le_map hmono (by simpa [f] using hx)
refine ⟨f^[m] x, ?_⟩
constructor
· simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using horbit (Nat.le_succ m)
· have hle : f^[m + 1] x ≤ f^[n] x := horbit (Nat.succ_le_of_lt hmn)
  have hback : f^[n] x ≤ f^[m] x := by
    simpa [f] using hrepeat.2
  exact le_trans (by simpa [f, Function.iterate_succ_apply'] using hle) hback

op_000055.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ boxk k h) ↔ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
op_000055.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ boxk k h) ↔ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
op_000055.statement_formalization_notes: Dropped the redundant local `boxk` abbreviation because it is already declared in the repository; kept the proposition otherwise unchanged.

op_000055.proof_sketch: The reverse implication is immediate: from `∃ h, h ≐ □h`, choose `k = 1`, use `0 < 1`, and identify `boxk 1 h` with `□h`. For the forward implication, the hypothesis only gives a periodic point for the monotone map `□`, namely `h ≐ boxk k h` for some `k > 0`. The nearest reusable theorem is `thm_finite_box_orbit_henkin_000032`, but it needs one element whose own `□`-orbit is finite and also satisfies `x ≤ □x`; periodicity of an unrelated `h` does not seem to provide that bridge. The axioms do give a pre-fixpoint `⊠⊤ ≤ □⊠⊤`, via `reft_le_prov_reft`, but I do not see a reason its orbit must be finite just because some other orbit is periodic. `thm_box_orbit_repeat_henkin_000042` also does not apply directly, since it additionally requires a Gödel fixpoint. So I can justify the easy direction, but I do not currently see a valid derivation or a fully verified refutation of the hard direction.
op_000055.counterexample_text: A concrete counterexample candidate is a finite preorder built from a Boolean-lattice-style `□` action that swaps two incomparable atoms, so one gets `boxk 2 h = h` for an atom `h` but no immediate equivalence `h ≐ □h` on that 2-cycle. The standard model is the powerset of `{1,2}` with subset order and `□` induced by the transposition `(1 2)`, where `{1}` is 2-periodic and not equivalent to `□{1} = {2}`. That model still has fixed points `∅` and `{1,2}`, so it does not refute the target as stated, but it shows why periodicity alone is weaker than a Henkin fixed point. A more serious refutation attempt would modify the preorder/top/reft structure so APS holds while all `□`-fixed points are destroyed, but I could not verify such a construction from the given axioms.

op_000055.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ boxk k h) ↔ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
op_000055.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h) ↔ ∃ h : α, h ≐ □h
op_000055.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized `boxk k h` as the `k`-fold function iterate `((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h`, which is the conservative parsing-compatible expansion of the statement.

op_000055.proof_sketch: The implication from right to left is immediate: given `∃ h, h ≐ □h`, take `k = 1`, note `0 < 1`, and use `((fun z : α => □z)^[1]) h = □h`. For the converse, the hypothesis only provides a `k`-periodic point up to `≐`, not a one-step Henkin fixed point. The nearest reusable theorem `thm_finite_box_orbit_henkin_000032` would be enough if we could exhibit some `x` with `x ≤ □x` and finite `□`-orbit, but from `h ≐ □^[k] h` I do not see a valid bridge to both requirements for the same element. The other derived bridge, `thm_box_orbit_repeat_henkin_000042`, also needs `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`, which is not assumed here. So only the easy direction is presently justified, and I cannot complete a proof or a verified refutation from the stated axioms alone.
op_000055.counterexample_text: A concrete counterexample candidate starts from a four-element finite preorder with bottom `⊥`, top `⊤`, and two incomparable middle points `a,b`, and lets `□` swap `a` and `b` while fixing `⊥,⊤`. Then `a ≐ □^2 a` holds but `a ≐ □a` fails, showing why periodicity is strictly weaker than a one-step fixed point. To turn this into a full refutation one would need an order-reversing `⊠` satisfying all APS axioms, for instance a near-constant map with `⊠x = ⊤` on most inputs and `⊠⊥ = ⊤`; however, I could not verify the remaining APS inequalities in such a model. So this is only a plausible model shape, not a confirmed counterexample.

op_000056.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a precise Lean proposition; I preserved its iterate notation and repository equivalence notation without introducing extra helpers.

op_000056.proof_sketch: The forward implication is straightforward with k = 1, x = h, m = 0, n = 1. The reverse implication appears to require collapsing a positive-iterate box-orbit repetition for f = (fun z => □z)^[k] to an actual Henkin fixed point h ≐ □h. With only the ACR/APS preorder axioms and prov_mono, the given data yields at best a box^k-periodic/equivalent point, but there is no available verified theorem in the provided context that converts such a box^k fixed point or repeated box^k orbit into a one-step Henkin fixed point. The related bridge problem op_000055 is still open/previously failed, so this route cannot presently be completed defensibly in Lean.

op_000056.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ h : α, h ≐ □h) ↔ ∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x ≐ (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)

op_000056.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition in the repository’s notation, so I preserved it verbatim with no prelude.

op_000056.proof_sketch: The forward implication is immediate: from `h ≐ □h`, take `k = 1`, `x = h`, `m = 0`, and `n = 1`. Then `0 < k`, the inequality `x ≤ ((fun z => □z)^[k]) x` is exactly `h ≤ □h`, and the repetition clause is exactly `h ≐ □h`. For the reverse implication, the hypothesis only gives a repeated orbit for `f := (fun z : α => □z)^[k]` up to `≐`, not an actual one-step fixed point for `□`. The closest reusable derived bridges, `thm_finite_box_orbit_henkin_000032` and the verified `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037`, would need a finite orbit stated with actual equality of iterates, while `thm_box_orbit_repeat_henkin_000042` additionally requires a Gödel fixpoint; none of these hypotheses are supplied here. So the missing bridge is still a theorem turning `□^k`-orbit repetition modulo `≐` into either equality-based orbit finiteness or directly into some `h ≐ □h`.
op_000056.counterexample_text: Concrete refutation direction: try a finite preorder with antisymmetry coming from equality and let `□` act as a nontrivial cycle, so some positive power `□^k` has periodic orbits but no point satisfies `x ≐ □x` unless `□` has a fixed point. Then attempt to choose `Reft` as a very degenerate map making the APS axioms hold as weakly as possible. I cannot verify that all APS axioms are simultaneously realizable in such a model, so this remains only a counterexample candidate, not a confirmed refutation.

op_000060.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], (∃ k : ℕ, 0 < k ∧ ∃ h : α, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)

op_000060.proof_sketch: The supplied counterexample direction is not defensible as stated. The proposed model is a 3-element type, hence finite. But the repository already contains `thm_finite_henkin_fixpoint_exists_000031`, which proves that for every finite `α` with `[ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α]`, one has `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`. Therefore no 3-element APS model can witness failure of the forward implication. In particular, even if `thm_constant_reft_iff_bounds_000044` supplies the constant `Reft`/`APS` structure for the proposed bounds, finiteness immediately forces a Henkin fixpoint, contradicting the intended refutation. I do not currently have a valid infinite countermodel or a complete proof of the statement, so the conservative formalization result is `stuck`.
op_000060.counterexample_text: The proposed counterexample cannot work in this theory context: it is finite, and `thm_finite_henkin_fixpoint_exists_000031` already shows every finite APS model has a Henkin fixpoint. So the 3-element construction cannot refute the biconditional.

The forward implication is the unresolved step. The currently verified derived theorems only let one recover another `□^k`-fixed point from finite/repeating `boxk`-orbit hypotheses, or a genuine Henkin fixpoint from repeating ordinary `□`-orbits together with an explicit hypothesis `x ≤ □x`. From `∃ k > 0, ∃ h, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h` alone, I do not see a verified lemma in `Derived.lean` that collapses a positive iterate fixed point to `h ≐ □h`, and the supplied sketch implicitly assumes exactly that missing iterate-collapse result.

The forward implication, however, only yields a `boxk k`-fixed point via the verified theorem `thm_boxk_orbit_repeat_fixpoint_000047` (or via finiteness and `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037`), namely `∃ h, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h`. The needed bridge from a positive iterate fixed point to a genuine Henkin fixed point `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)` is exactly the still-missing content of open problem `op_000060`; it is not derivable from the currently verified lemmas in `Derived.lean`, and the supplied sketch assumes that missing collapse lemma.

op_000061.proof_sketch: The backward implication is plausible: from a Henkin fixed point `h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α`, take `x := h.1`; since `h.2` gives `x ≐ □x`, monotonicity of `□` lets one iterate to obtain `x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x` for any `k > 0`. Then choosing `m := 0` and `n := 1` gives the required repetition for the `((fun z : α => □z)^[k])`-orbit, because `x` is equivalent to every positive iterate of `□`. The forward implication is the obstruction: the hypothesis only gives eventual repetition for the map `F := (fun z : α => □z)^[k]` together with `x ≤ F x`. The nearest reusable result is `thm_finite_box_orbit_henkin_000032`, but it needs finiteness of the full `□`-orbit and a one-step bound `x ≤ □x`, neither of which follows directly from repetition of the `F`-orbit. So the missing bridge is exactly a theorem turning an `F`-periodic or finite `F`-orbit point for some `k > 0` into a genuine Henkin fixed point, and no such theorem is present in the supplied derived list.
op_000061.counterexample_text: Concrete counterexample candidate: a finite preorder where `□` acts on some equivalence classes as a nontrivial cycle of length `k > 1`, so `((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ≐ x` and the left-hand side holds for that `x`, but no element satisfies `x ≐ □x`. The unresolved point is whether one can realize such a cycle while also defining `⊠` so that all `ACR.APS` axioms hold; I cannot verify that compatibility from the given facts alone.

op_000071.proof_sketch: Instantiate a four-element preorder with elements `t,c,a,b`, order generated by reflexivity plus `t ≤ c` and `a ≤ c`, constant provability `□x = c`, and refutability map `⊠t = c`, `⊠c = a`, `⊠a = a`, `⊠b = c`. Then `a` is a Gödel fixpoint, the model is consistent since `t ≰ b`, and `⊠⊠⊤ = a` is not equivalent to `⊠⊤ = c` because `c ≰ a`.

op_000076.proof_sketch: Use Mathlib’s iterate-order equivalence with `id` and the monotonicity of `□`: for `k > 0`, `h ≤ ((fun z => □z)^[k]) h` iff `h ≤ □h`, and similarly `((fun z => □z)^[k]) h ≤ h` iff `□h ≤ h`. Thus a `□^[k]`-fixed point is exactly a Henkin fixpoint, and conversely any Henkin fixpoint is fixed by every positive iterate.
op_000076.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ k hk
constructor
· rintro ⟨h, hh⟩
  let f : α → α := fun z => □z
  have hmono : Monotone f := by
    intro a b hab
    exact ACR.prov_mono hab
  have h₁ : h ≤ f h := by
    exact ((Function.Commute.id_left (f := f)).iterate_pos_le_iff_map_le'
      strictMono_id hmono (x := h) (n := k) hk).mp (by simpa [f] using hh.1)
  have h₂ : f h ≤ h := by
    exact ((Function.Commute.id_right (f := f)).iterate_pos_le_iff_map_le
      hmono strictMono_id (x := h) (n := k) hk).mp (by simpa [f] using hh.2)
  exact ⟨⟨h, by simpa [f] using And.intro h₁ h₂⟩⟩
· rintro ⟨h⟩
  let f : α → α := fun z => □z
  have hmono : Monotone f := by
    intro a b hab
    exact ACR.prov_mono hab
  have h₁ : h.1 ≤ f^[k] h.1 := by
    exact ((Function.Commute.id_left (f := f)).iterate_pos_le_iff_map_le'
      strictMono_id hmono (x := h.1) (n := k) hk).mpr (by simpa [f] using h.2.1)
  have h₂ : f^[k] h.1 ≤ h.1 := by
    exact ((Function.Commute.id_right (f := f)).iterate_pos_le_iff_map_le
      hmono strictMono_id (x := h.1) (n := k) hk).mpr (by simpa [f] using h.2.2)
  exact ⟨h.1, by simpa [f] using And.intro h₁ h₂⟩

op_000076.proof_sketch: The supplied proof route is not defensible in the current ACR/APS theory. It implicitly uses order laws such as `x ≤ ⊤`, but `ACR` only extends `Top`/`Bot` as elements and does not assume `⊤` is greatest or `⊥` is least, so the argument that `⊤ ≐ □⊤` fails. I also checked the verified generated lemmas: `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037` only produces a `□^[k]`-fixed point `∃ h, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h`, not a genuine Henkin point `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`, and no listed theorem collapses a positive-iterate fixed point to a one-step `h ≐ □h`. So the hard implication remains exactly the missing positive-iterate-collapse bridge, and I do not have a sound Lean proof or a verified counterexample from the available context.

op_000077.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ (((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x) ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)))

op_000077.proof_sketch: The forward implication looks formalizable from a Henkin fixpoint by taking the witness itself and using monotonicity to show it is equivalent to every positive iterate. The reverse implication in the supplied sketch is not justified by the available library facts: `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037` only yields `∃ h, h ≐ ((fun z => □z)^[k]) h`, not `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)`, and I could not find a valid preorder-level lemma collapsing a positive `boxk`-fixed point to a `□`-fixed point. So the stated route cannot currently be completed in Lean without an additional theorem such as op_000076.

op_000077.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ ∃ m n : ℕ, m < n ∧ (((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[m]) x) ≐ ((((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x)))

op_000077.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition; I preserved its iterate notation and repository equivalence notation unchanged.

op_000077.proof_sketch: The forward implication is still the easy half: from `h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α`, take `x := h.1`, use `h.2` and monotonicity of `□` to propagate `h.1 ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h.1` for any `k > 0`, and then choose `m := 0`, `n := 1`. The real issue is the reverse implication. The nearest reusable bridge is the verified `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037`, but the present hypothesis only gives a repeat up to `≐` along the `box^k`-orbit, not finiteness of the actual orbit set as required there. In a preorder, `a ≐ b` does not imply `a = b`, so eventual repetition modulo `≐` is too weak to collapse the orbit to a finite set of equal elements. I also do not see any listed theorem turning a periodic point of `((fun z => □z)^[k])` with `k > 0` into a genuine one-step Henkin fixpoint for `□`. So I cannot justify the stated equivalence from the available axioms and derived facts.
op_000077.counterexample_text: A plausible countermodel direction is a non-antisymmetric preorder with infinitely many distinct representatives inside the same `≐`-classes, and a monotone `□` that shifts representatives so that some `x` satisfies `x ≤ ((fun z => □z)^[k]) x` and two `box^k` iterates are `≐`-equivalent, while no element is actually `≐`-fixed by one application of `□`. This would make the right-hand side true without giving the finiteness/equality input needed for `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037`. I have not fully checked the APS axioms for such a structure, so this remains a candidate refutation direction rather than a verified counterexample.

op_000082.proof_sketch: Use the two-point Boolean preorder model from the sketch: `false ≤ _` and `_ ≤ true`, with `⊤ = true`, `⊥ = false`, `□ = id`, and `⊠ = Bool.not`. The APS and C5 axioms are verified by finite case splits. Consistency is immediate since `true ≤ false` is false, there is no Gödel fixpoint because neither Boolean is equivalent to its negation in this preorder, and every positive iterate of `□` is still the identity, so `false` is a fixed point for all `k > 0`.
op_000082.prelude_code:
def BoolLe : Bool → Bool → Prop := fun a b => a = false ∨ b = true

def boolProv : Bool → Bool := fun x => x

def boolReft : Bool → Bool := fun x => !x

def boolACR : ACR Bool where
  top := true
  bot := false
  le := BoolLe
  refl := by
    intro a
    cases a <;> simp [BoolLe]
  trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    cases a <;> cases b <;> cases c <;> simp [BoolLe] at hab hbc ⊢

def boolProvInst : @ACR.Prov Bool boolACR where
  prov := boolProv

def boolReftInst : @ACR.Reft Bool boolACR where
  reft := boolReft

def boolAPS : @ACR.APS Bool boolACR boolProvInst boolReftInst where
  prov_mono := by
    intro x y h
    simpa [boolProv] using h
  reft_anti_mono := by
    intro x y h
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [BoolLe, boolReft] at h ⊢
  top_le_reft_bot := by
    simp [BoolLe, boolReft]
  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
    intro x y hxy hxr
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp [BoolLe, boolProv, boolReft] at hxy hxr ⊢
  reft_le_prov_reft := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> simp [BoolLe, boolProv, boolReft]

def boolC5 : @ACR.C5 Bool boolACR boolProvInst boolReftInst boolAPS where
  le_top := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> simp [BoolLe]
op_000082.proof_text:
refine ⟨Bool, boolACR, boolProvInst, boolReftInst, boolAPS, boolC5, ?_⟩
constructor
· show ¬ ((⊤ : Bool) ≤ (⊥ : Bool))
  simp [BoolLe]
constructor
· intro h
  rcases h with ⟨⟨g, hg⟩⟩
  cases g <;> simp [ACR.Equivalent, BoolLe, boolReft] at hg
· intro k hk
  refine ⟨false, ?_⟩
  constructor <;> simpa [boolProv]

deriving DecidableEq

    | .tt, .tt => True
    | .tt, .ff => False

  top := .tt

instance : Bot Two where
  bot := .ff

instance : Preorder Two where
  le := (· ≤ ·)
  le_refl := by
    intro x
    cases x <;> trivial
  le_trans := by
    intro a b c hab hbc
    cases a <;> cases b <;> cases c <;> trivial

  toBot := ULift.instBot
  toPreorder := ULift.instPreorder

instance : ACR.Reft (ULift Two) where

    intro x y h
    simpa using h

    intro x y h

  le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by

    cases x using ULift.casesOn with

  reft_le_prov_reft := by

    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases x <;> trivial

instance : ACR.C5 (ULift Two) where
  le_top := by
    intro x
    cases x using ULift.casesOn with
    | _ x =>
      cases x <;> trivial

op_000083.proof_sketch: Refute the universal implication by instantiating it with a concrete three-point preorder. Take a chain `zero < one < two`, but declare `top = one` and `bot = zero`. Let `□x := two` for all `x`, and let `⊠` be `zero ↦ two`, `one ↦ two`, `two ↦ zero`. Then the APS axioms hold: monotonicity of `□` is trivial, antitonicity of `⊠` is checked by cases, `top ≤ ⊠bot` is `one ≤ two`, the mixed axiom always concludes `x ≤ ⊠top = two`, and `⊠x ≤ □⊠x` is always `⊠x ≤ two`. The model is consistent because `one ≤ zero` is false. Also `¬⊬ ⊠⊤` holds because `⊠⊤ = two` and `two ≤ zero` is false. But there is no Gödel fixpoint: `zero ≐ ⊠zero` would require `two ≤ zero`, `one ≐ ⊠one` would require `two ≤ one`, and `two ≐ ⊠two` would require `two ≤ zero`; all are impossible. Hence the target universal statement is false.

  decide

Then `Consistent` holds since `one ≤ zero` is false, and `¬⊬ ⊠⊤` holds since `⊠⊤ = two` and `two ≤ zero` is false. But there is no Gödel fixpoint: `zero` and `one` both map to `two`, while `two` maps to `zero`, so no element is equivalent to its `⊠`-image.

The model is consistent since `⊤ ≤ ⊥` means `t = b`, false. Also `¬⊬ ⊠⊤` holds because `⊠⊤ = c`, so refutability would require `c = b`, false. op_000086.proof_sketch: Use the finite chain `Fin (2 * m)` with its usual order, explicit bottom `b = 0` and top `t = 2*m-1`. Let `□` be the identity. Let `⊠b = t` and `⊠x = b` for every `x ≠ b`. Then APS holds: antitonicity is immediate on a chain, `top_le_reft_bot` is `t ≤ t`, the mixed APS axiom reduces either to `x ≤ b` from `x ≤ y` when `y = b` or directly from the second premise when `y ≠ b`, and `reft_le_prov_reft` is reflexivity because `□ = id`. Consistency is `¬ t ≤ b`. If a Gödel fixpoint existed, `ACR.irrefutable_reft_top` would imply `¬ (⊠t ≤ b)`, but here `⊠t = b`, contradiction. Finally `x ≐ □x` is reflexivity for every `x`.
op_000086.proof_text:
intro m hm
  have h2m : 0 < 2 * m := by
    omega
  let α := Fin (2 * m)
  let b : α := ⟨0, h2m⟩
  let t : α := ⟨2 * m - 1, by
    omega⟩
  let provFn : α → α := fun x => x
  let reftFn : α → α := fun x => if x = b then t else b
  letI : Fintype α := inferInstance
  letI : ACR α :=
    { top := t
      bot := b
      le := fun x y => x.val ≤ y.val
      refl := by
        intro x
        exact le_rfl
      trans := by
        intro x y z hxy hyz
        exact le_trans hxy hyz }
  have hb_le_t : b ≤ t := by
    change b.val ≤ t.val
    dsimp [b, t]
    omega
  have ht_ne_b : t ≠ b := by
    intro h
    have hval : t.val = b.val := by
      simpa using congrArg Fin.val h
    dsimp [b, t] at hval
    omega
  letI : ACR.Prov α := ⟨provFn⟩
  letI : ACR.Reft α := ⟨reftFn⟩
  letI : ACR.APS α :=
    { prov_mono := by
        intro x y hxy
        simpa [provFn] using hxy
      reft_anti_mono := by
        intro x y hxy
        by_cases hx : x = b
        · by_cases hy : y = b
          · simp [reftFn, hx, hy]
          · simpa [reftFn, hx, hy] using hb_le_t
        · have hy : y ≠ b := by
            intro hy
            apply hx
            apply Fin.ext
            have hxle : x.val ≤ b.val := by
              simpa [hy] using hxy
            dsimp [b] at hxle
            omega
          simp [reftFn, hx, hy]
      top_le_reft_bot := by
        simp [reftFn]
      le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
        intro x y hxy hxr
        by_cases hy : y = b
        · simpa [provFn, reftFn, hy, ht_ne_b] using hxy
        · simpa [provFn, reftFn, hy, ht_ne_b] using hxr
      reft_le_prov_reft := by
        intro x
        simpa [provFn] using (show reftFn x ≤ reftFn x from le_rfl) }
  letI : ACR.C5 α :=
    { le_top := by
        intro x
        change x.val ≤ t.val
        dsimp [t]
        omega }
  have hcons : ACR.Consistent α := by
    intro hinc
    change t.val ≤ b.val at hinc
    dsimp [b, t] at hinc
    omega
  have hno : ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := by
    intro hg
    letI : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := hg
    have hirr : ¬ ((⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ (⊥ : α)) := ACR.irrefutable_reft_top (α := α) hcons
    apply hirr
    simpa [reftFn, ht_ne_b]
  refine ⟨α, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨hcons, ?_⟩
  refine ⟨by
    simpa [α], ?_⟩
  refine ⟨hno, ?_⟩
  intro x
  constructor <;> simp [provFn]

Then `APS` is proved by explicit `change` steps that unfold `provFn` and `reftFn`; consistency is `¬ t ≤ b`; nonexistence of Gödel fixpoints follows from `ACR.irrefutable_reft_top`; and `x ≐ □x` is reflexivity because `□ = id`.

have h2m : 0 < 2 * m := by

let α := ULift (Fin (2 * m))

let t : α := ⟨⟨2 * m - 1, by
  omega⟩⟩
let provFn : α → α := fun x => x
let reftFn : α → α := fun x => if x = b then t else b
letI : Fintype α := inferInstance
letI : ACR α :=
  { toTop := ⟨t⟩
    toBot := ⟨b⟩
    toPreorder := inferInstance }
have hb_le_t : b ≤ t := by
  change b.down.val ≤ t.down.val
  dsimp [b, t]
  omega
have ht_ne_b : t ≠ b := by
  intro h
  have hval : t.down.val = b.down.val := by
    simpa using congrArg (fun z : α => z.down.val) h

  omega
letI : ACR.Prov α := ⟨provFn⟩
letI : ACR.Reft α := ⟨reftFn⟩
letI : ACR.APS α :=
  { prov_mono := by
      intro x y hxy

    reft_anti_mono := by
      intro x y hxy

    top_le_reft_bot := by
      change t ≤ reftFn b
      simp [reftFn]
    le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
      intro x y hxy hxr

op_000087.proof_sketch: The reverse implication is straightforward: a Henkin fixed point stays equivalent to every positive iterate of `□` by induction. The forward implication is the blocker. The available derived lemmas produce either a `boxk k`-fixed point from a finite `boxk k`-orbit, or a Henkin point from an inflationary one-step box orbit (`x ≤ □x`) with a repeated iterate. From `x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x`, I can get a finite `boxk k`-orbit and hence `∃ h, h ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h`, but I could not derive the needed one-step inflationary hypothesis `h ≤ □h` or an equivalent bridge to `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)` using only verified repo lemmas. A small brute-force search over all 2- and 3-point finite APS models did not reveal a counterexample, so I am not returning `counterexample`, but I do not have a defensible Lean proof path for the forward direction.

op_000087.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ((∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
op_000087.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → ((∃ x : α, x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x) ↔ Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α))
op_000087.statement_formalization_notes: Used the repository notation `≐` and kept the iterate expression exactly as in the target statement for conservative formalization.

op_000087.proof_sketch: The backward implication looks standard: from `h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α` we have `h.1 ≐ □h.1`, and monotonicity of `□` lets one inductively propagate this to `h.1 ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) h.1` for every `k > 0`. For the forward implication, the nearest reusable bridge is the derived theorem `thm_finite_boxk_orbit_henkin_000037` mentioned in the theory context, since `x ≐ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x` gives the inflationary half `x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x`. The missing bridge is finiteness of the `boxk k`-orbit in the theorem’s hypothesis: `≐` gives only mutual `≤`, not literal equality of iterates, so it does not force the orbit set `{y | ∃ n, ((fun z => □z)^[k])^[n] x = y}` to be finite. The other available Henkin-producing lemmas likewise demand extra data, such as a finite one-step orbit or a Gödel fixed point with repeated equal iterates, and I do not see how to derive those from the target assumption alone. So I do not have a defensible proof path, but I also do not have a verified counterexample.
