op_000096.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)))
op_000096.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a well-formed Lean proposition using repository notation, so I preserved it verbatim.

op_000096.proof_sketch: The closest reusable result is `thm_godel_fixpoint_iff_reft_000002`, but it needs `[ACR.C5 α]`, which the target does not assume. Under the present assumptions, `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` gives only the forward inequality `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α)` via `ACR.reft_reft_top_le_reft_top`, and consistency only yields `¬((⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ ⊥)` via `ACR.irrefutable_reft_top`. The reverse inequality `⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α)` is exactly the missing bridge that `C5` supplies in `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top`, so I do not see how to prove the equivalence from the stated hypotheses alone. Conversely, none of the provided theorems derives a Gödel fixpoint from `⊠⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊤` without adding stronger existence assumptions such as finiteness. So the statement still looks underpowered rather than provable from the available toolkit.
op_000096.counterexample_text: Concrete refutation candidate: use a finite preorder where the distinguished `⊤` is not actually greatest, so `C5` fails but consistency holds. Let the carrier have elements `b < c < t` and another element `u` incomparable with `t`, take `⊥ = b`, `⊤ = t`, define `⊠x = c` for all `x`, and define `□x = t` for all `x`; then `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)` is automatic since both are `c`, while there is no `g` with `g ≐ ⊠g = c` if every element except `c` is inequivalent to `c` and `c` itself is arranged to fail the Gödel-fixpoint condition through the preorder shape. I have not checked every APS axiom in this model, so this is only a concrete counterexample direction, not a certified countermodel.

op_000096.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)))
op_000096.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → (Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)))
op_000096.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition using repository notation, so I preserved it unchanged.

op_000096.proof_sketch: The reverse implication is actually easy: from `h : ⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α)`, take `g := ⊠(⊤ : α)` and swap the two inequalities in `h` to get `g ≐ ⊠g`, hence `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)`. The real difficulty is the forward implication. From a Gödel fixpoint, the theory file only gives `ACR.reft_reft_top_le_reft_top`, i.e. `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊠(⊤ : α)`, and consistency gives only the irrefutability statement `ACR.irrefutable_reft_top`, not the missing reverse inequality. The nearest packaged equivalence is `thm_godel_fixpoint_iff_reft_000002`, but it explicitly needs `[ACR.C5 α]`, and `C5` is exactly what lets `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top` identify an arbitrary Gödel fixpoint with `⊠⊤`. So I do not see a proof of `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α))` from the stated assumptions alone.
op_000096.counterexample_text: Concrete refutation direction: look for a finite preorder where the designated `⊤` is not actually greatest, so `[ACR.C5 α]` fails but `ACR.Consistent α` still holds. A plausible shape is four elements `b, g, t, r` with `b ≤ g ≤ t ≤ r`, designated constants `⊥ = b`, `⊤ = t`, and a reft map with `⊠g = g` (so `g` is a Gödel fixpoint) but `⊠t = b` and `⊠b = r`, which makes `⊠⊠(⊤ : α) = r` and `⊠(⊤ : α) = b`, hence not equivalent. One then tries to choose `□` so that `g` is provable enough for the APS bridge axiom while keeping consistency `¬(t ≤ b)`. I have not fully verified all APS axioms for this model, so this is a concrete counterexample candidate rather than a certified countermodel.

op_000099.source_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y})
op_000099.formalized_statement: ∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] (k : ℕ), 0 < k → (Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≤ ((fun z : α => □z)^[k]) x ∧ Set.Finite {y : α | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z : α => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y})
op_000099.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition; I preserved its iterate notation and explicit quantification unchanged.

op_000099.proof_sketch: The forward implication is not valid in general: a Henkin fixpoint only gives equivalence h.1 ≐ □h.1 in a preorder, not equality h.1 = □h.1. Since the orbit condition is stated using equality of iterates, one can have a Henkin fixpoint while the actual iterate orbit is infinite.
op_000099.counterexample_text: Take α := ℤ with the indiscrete preorder x ≤ y defined to be True for all x,y, so ACR/APS axioms are trivial. Let ⊤, ⊥ be arbitrary elements, let □x := x + 1, and let ⊠ be any function (for instance identity). Then every pair of elements is equivalent, so for any x we have x ≐ □x; hence Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) holds. But for any k > 0 and any x, the map ((fun z => □z)^[k]) is x ↦ x + k, so its iterates are x + nk. The set {y | ∃ n, (((fun z => □z)^[k])^[n]) x = y} = {x + nk | n : ℕ} is infinite. Thus the existential finite-orbit condition fails for every x, while the Henkin-fixpoint side is true. Therefore the stated equivalence is false in general.
op_000099.lean_error_excerpt: Lean verification succeeded but proof still contains sorry

op_000099.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement at k = 1 using the type ULift Nat with the indiscrete preorder x ≤ y := True, identity reflection, and provability operator □x := x + 1. In this model every element is automatically Henkin because x ≐ □x is trivial in the preorder, so the left side holds. But the equality-orbit set from any x is exactly {x + n | n : Nat}, which is infinite; a finite-orbit witness would therefore give an injective map Nat → finite set, impossible.

op_000099.proof_text:
intro h
have hHenkin : Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint (ULift ℕ)) := by
  refine ⟨⟨(⟨0⟩ : ULift ℕ), ?_⟩⟩
  constructor <;> trivial
have hiff := h (α := ULift ℕ) 1 Nat.one_pos
rcases hiff.mp hHenkin with ⟨x, hx, hfin⟩
let s : Set (ULift ℕ) := {y : ULift ℕ | ∃ n : ℕ, (((fun z : ULift ℕ => □z)^[1])^[n]) x = y}
have hsfin : s.Finite := by
  simpa [s] using hfin
have hiter : ∀ n : ℕ, ((fun z : ULift ℕ => □z)^[n]) x = ⟨x.down + n⟩ := by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      rfl
  | succ n ih =>
      rw [Function.iterate_succ_apply']
      rw [ih]
      rfl
classical
letI := hsfin.fintype
let g : ℕ → s := fun n => ⟨⟨x.down + n⟩, ⟨n, by
  simpa [Function.iterate_mul] using hiter n⟩⟩
have hg_inj : Function.Injective g := by
  intro m n hmn
  have hvals : x.down + m = x.down + n := by
    exact congrArg (fun z : s => z.1.down) hmn
  exact Nat.add_left_cancel hvals
exact (not_injective_infinite_finite g) hg_inj
op_000099.counterexample_text: Take α = ULift Nat. Define the preorder by x ≤ y :≡ True for all x,y, let ⊤ = ⊥ = 0, let □x := x + 1, and let ⊠x := x. Then all ACR/Prov/Reft/APS axioms hold trivially because the order is indiscrete. The left side of the target equivalence is true: for example x = 0 is a Henkin fixpoint since both inequalities in x ≐ □x are automatic. Now specialize the claimed theorem to k = 1. For any x, the orbit set in the right side is {y | ∃ n, ((fun z => □z)^[n]) x = y} = {x + n | n : Nat}, because iterating □ just adds 1 each time. This set is infinite, so it cannot be finite for any x. Hence the existential finite-orbit condition is false while Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) is true. Therefore the universal equivalence is false.

op_000103.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by instantiating it with a concrete three-element ACR model. Take a type with elements `a,b,c`, preorder given by equality, `⊤ = a`, `⊥ = b`, `□ = id`, and `⊠` the 3-cycle `a ↦ c`, `b ↦ a`, `c ↦ b`. Then the APS axioms hold: `prov_mono` and `reft_le_prov_reft` are trivial because `□ = id`; `reft_anti_mono` is trivial under equality order; `top_le_reft_bot` is `a = ⊠b`; and `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` is vacuous because its hypotheses force `y = ⊠ y`, but the 3-cycle has no fixed points. The model is consistent since `a ≠ b`, and `¬ (⊬ ⊠⊤)` holds since `⊠⊤ = c` and `c ≠ b`. If the target theorem were true, we would get a Gödel fixpoint `g` with `g ≐ ⊠ g`, which under equality order means `g = ⊠ g`; case analysis on `g` contradicts the 3-cycle definition.

op_000107.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a precise Lean proposition; I kept the repository notation and explicit existential instance binders.

op_000107.proof_sketch: The proposed refutation at n = 2 is not defensible from the available axioms. `IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` only says there is no element `g` with `g ≐ ⊠g`; it does not force `⊠` to be a swap on a two-element carrier, and the theory does not assume antisymmetry, totality, or any classification of 2-point preorders. Likewise, `top_le_reft_bot : ⊤ ≤ ⊠⊥` does not identify `⊠⊥` with `⊤`, so one cannot derive `⊠⊤ ≤ ⊥` from cardinality alone. I do not see a sound Lean refutation of the universal statement from the given context.
op_000107.counterexample_text: The intended counterexample argument relies on extra structural assumptions not present in the theory: it treats a finite two-element preorder as if it were a two-element antisymmetric chain and treats absence of Gödel fixpoints as forcing permutation-like behavior of `⊠`. Neither step follows from the stated axioms, so the universal statement cannot be refuted this way.

op_000107.source_statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 2 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ ¬ (⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α)) ∧ IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n
op_000107.formalized_statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 2 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ ¬ (⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α)) ∧ IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n
op_000107.statement_formalization_notes: The natural-language target is already a valid Lean proposition using repository notation, so I preserved it verbatim.

op_000107.proof_sketch: The supplied refutation at `n = 2` is not derivable from the stated axioms. `Fintype.card α = 2` plus `ACR.Consistent α` does not classify the preorder on `α` as exactly two distinct points `⊥, ⊤` in a way strong enough to force `⊠` to swap them, because there is no antisymmetry or linearity assumption in the target. Likewise, `IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` only rules out elements satisfying `g ≐ ⊠g`; it does not determine the values of `⊠⊥` and `⊠⊤`. I checked the verified theory context: there is an all-size family theorem `thm_finite_linear_no_godel_family_000110` giving models with `IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` for every `n ≥ 2`, but it does not provide the extra conjunct `¬ (⊬ ⊠(⊤ : α))`. The library also contains `thm_godel_exists_from_irrefutable_reft_top_000103_is_false`, showing that consistency together with `¬ (⊬ ⊠⊤)` does not force a Gödel fixpoint, so there is no obvious way to upgrade the existing family theorem to the exact target. With the current context, I do not have a sound Lean proof of either the statement or its negation.
op_000107.counterexample_text: The proposed counterexample direction relies on non-axiomatic structure. In this theory, a two-element finite carrier need not let us conclude that the only elements are forced to behave as a Boolean chain in a way that determines `⊠`. Absence of Gödel fixpoints also does not imply that `⊠` must be the transposition `⊥ ↦ ⊤`, `⊤ ↦ ⊥`. Therefore the suggested contradiction `⊬ ⊠⊤` cannot be formally justified from the given assumptions alone.

op_000110.source_statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 2 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) ∧ IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n
op_000110.formalized_statement: ∀ n : ℕ, 2 ≤ n → ∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) ∧ IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Fintype.card α = n
op_000110.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition; I preserved it verbatim apart from relying on repository notation `≐` and `□` already present in the statement.

op_000110.proof_sketch: Use the same `ULift (Fin k)` Gödel-free construction already verified for even sizes, but instantiate it directly at arbitrary `n ≥ 2`. Take `□ = id`, let `⊥` be `0`, `⊤` be `n - 1`, and define `⊠x = ⊤` at `⊥` and `⊠x = ⊥` otherwise. The APS axioms are checked exactly as in the derived even-cardinality family. Consistency follows from `⊤ ≰ ⊥`, there are no Gödel fixpoints by `irrefutable_reft_top`, totality and antisymmetry come from the linear order on `Fin n`, and the cardinality is `n`.
op_000110.proof_text:
intro n hn
have hpos : 0 < n := by
  omega
let α := ULift (Fin n)
let b : α := ⟨⟨0, hpos⟩⟩
let t : α := ⟨⟨n - 1, by
  omega⟩⟩
let provFn : α → α := fun x => x
let reftFn : α → α := fun x => if x = b then t else b
letI : Fintype α := inferInstance
letI : ACR α :=
  { toTop := ⟨t⟩
    toBot := ⟨b⟩
    toPreorder := inferInstance }
have hb_le_t : b ≤ t := by
  change b.down.val ≤ t.down.val
  dsimp [b, t]
  omega
have ht_ne_b : t ≠ b := by
  intro h
  have hval : t.down.val = b.down.val := by
    simpa using congrArg (fun z : α => z.down.val) h
  dsimp [b, t] at hval
  omega
letI : ACR.Prov α := ⟨provFn⟩
letI : ACR.Reft α := ⟨reftFn⟩
letI : ACR.APS α :=
  { prov_mono := by
      intro x y hxy
      change provFn x ≤ provFn y
      simpa [provFn] using hxy
    reft_anti_mono := by
      intro x y hxy
      change reftFn y ≤ reftFn x
      by_cases hx : x = b
      · by_cases hy : y = b
        · simp [reftFn, hx, hy]
        · simp [reftFn, hx, hy, hb_le_t]
      · have hy : y ≠ b := by
          intro hy
          apply hx
          apply ULift.ext
          apply Fin.ext
          have hxle : x ≤ b := by
            simpa [hy] using hxy
          change x.down.val = b.down.val
          change x.down.val ≤ b.down.val at hxle
          dsimp [b] at hxle ⊢
          omega
        simp [reftFn, hx, hy]
    top_le_reft_bot := by
      change t ≤ reftFn b
      simp [reftFn]
    le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
      intro x y hxy hxr
      change x ≤ provFn y at hxy
      change x ≤ reftFn y at hxr
      change x ≤ reftFn t
      by_cases hy : y = b
      · have hxb : x ≤ b := by
          simpa [provFn, hy] using hxy
        simpa [reftFn, ht_ne_b] using hxb
      · have hxb : x ≤ b := by
          simpa [reftFn, hy] using hxr
        simpa [reftFn, ht_ne_b] using hxb
    reft_le_prov_reft := by
      intro x
      change reftFn x ≤ provFn (reftFn x)
      simp [provFn] }
letI : ACR.C5 α :=
  { le_top := by
      intro x
      change x.down.val ≤ t.down.val
      dsimp [t]
      omega }
have hcons : ACR.Consistent α := by
  intro hinc
  change t.down.val ≤ b.down.val at hinc
  dsimp [b, t] at hinc
  omega
have hno : ¬ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := by
  intro hg
  letI : Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := hg
  have hirr : ¬ ((⊠(⊤ : α)) ≤ (⊥ : α)) := ACR.irrefutable_reft_top (α := α) hcons
  apply hirr
  change reftFn t ≤ b
  simp [reftFn, ht_ne_b]
have hempty : IsEmpty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := not_nonempty_iff.mp hno
refine ⟨α, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, inferInstance, ?_⟩
refine ⟨hcons, ?_⟩
refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
· intro x
  constructor <;> change x ≤ provFn x <;> simp [provFn]
refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
· intro x y
  exact le_total x y
refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
· intro x y hxy hyx
  exact le_antisymm hxy hyx
refine ⟨hempty, ?_⟩
simpa [α] using Fintype.card_ulift (Fin n)

op_000109.proof_sketch: Specialize the universal claim at n = 1. From the returned witness type α with Fintype.card α = 1, deduce Subsingleton α via Fintype.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton. Then ⊥ = ⊤ by subsingletonity, so reflexivity gives (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥. That is exactly ACR.Inconsistent α, contradicting the assumed ACR.Consistent α.
op_000109.proof_text:
intro h
specialize h 1 le_rfl
rcases h with ⟨α, _, _, _, _, _, _, hcons, _, _, _, _, hcard⟩
have hsub : Subsingleton α :=
  (Fintype.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton).mp (by simpa [hcard])
haveI : Subsingleton α := hsub
have hincon : ACR.Inconsistent α := by
  change ((⊤ : α) ≤ (⊥ : α))
  rw [show (⊥ : α) = (⊤ : α) from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
  exact le_rfl
exact hcons hincon
op_000109.counterexample_text: Refute the statement at n = 1. Any finite type α with Fintype.card α = 1 is subsingleton, so ⊥ = ⊤. Reflexivity of the preorder then yields (⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥, i.e. ACR.Inconsistent α. Hence ACR.Consistent α cannot hold, so the existential package required by the statement does not exist for n = 1.

rw [Fintype.card_eq_one_iff] at hcard
rcases hcard with ⟨x, hx⟩

have hbot : (⊥ : α) = x := hx _

op_000116.source_statement: ∀ n : ℕ, (∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Fintype.card α = n) ↔ 2 ≤ n
op_000116.formalized_statement: ∀ n : ℕ, (∃ (α : Type*) (_ : Fintype α) (_ : ACR α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ Fintype.card α = n) ↔ 2 ≤ n
op_000116.statement_formalization_notes: The natural-language target is already a valid Lean proposition over the given theory context, so the formalization is kept verbatim.

op_000116.proof_sketch: For `→`, a witness `α` with `ACR α` is nonempty because `⊤ : α` exists, so `Fintype.card α ≠ 0`. If `Fintype.card α = 1`, then `α` is subsingleton, hence `⊤ = ⊥`, giving inconsistency; therefore `n ≠ 0` and `n ≠ 1`, so `2 ≤ n`. For `←`, apply `AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_finite_linear_no_godel_family_000110` and forget the extra structure.
op_000116.proof_text:
intro n
constructor
· rintro ⟨α, hF, hA, hcons, hcard⟩
  letI : Fintype α := hF
  letI : ACR α := hA
  have hzero : n ≠ 0 := by
    intro hn0
    letI : Nonempty α := ⟨⊤⟩
    have hne : Fintype.card α ≠ 0 := Fintype.card_ne_zero
    apply hne
    simpa [hcard] using hn0
  have hone : n ≠ 1 := by
    intro hn1
    have hsub : Subsingleton α :=
      (Fintype.card_le_one_iff_subsingleton).mp (by simpa [hcard, hn1])
    letI : Subsingleton α := hsub
    apply hcons
    change ((⊤ : α) ≤ (⊥ : α))
    rw [show (⊥ : α) = (⊤ : α) from Subsingleton.elim _ _]
  omega
· intro hn
  rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_finite_linear_no_godel_family_000110 n hn with
    ⟨α, hF, hA, hP, hR, hAPS, hC5, hcons, hbox, hlin, hanti, hnog, hcard⟩
  exact ⟨α, hF, hA, hcons, hcard⟩

op_000117.source_statement: Any bounded preorder with a greatest element admits a canonical trivial-`□`/constant-`⊠` realization: taking `□x = x` and `⊠x = ⊤` yields the APS axioms, and if `¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥)` then the resulting structure is consistent and has a Gödel fixed point.
op_000117.formalized_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α], (∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α)) → ∃ (P : ACR.Prov α) (R : ACR.Reft α) (_ : @ACR.APS α _ P R), (∀ x : α, P.prov x = x) ∧ (∀ x : α, R.reft x = (⊤ : α)) ∧ (¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ (⊥ : α)) → ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (@ACR.GödelFixpoint α _ R))
op_000117.statement_formalization_notes: Replaced the problematic `[ACR.C5 α]` binder with the explicit greatest-element hypothesis `∀ x, x ≤ ⊤`, which avoids circular typeclass synthesis while preserving the intended meaning.

op_000117.proof_sketch: Instantiate `Prov` by the identity and `Reft` by the constant-top map. The APS fields are immediate: `prov_mono` is the given order monotonicity, `reft_anti_mono`, `top_le_reft_bot`, and `reft_le_prov_reft` reduce to reflexivity on `⊤`, and `le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft` follows from the hypothesis `∀ x, x ≤ ⊤`. For the final implication, `ACR.Consistent α` is definitionally `¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥)`, and a Gödel fixpoint is witnessed by `⊤` since `⊠⊤ = ⊤`.
op_000117.proof_text:
intro α _ htop
refine ⟨{ prov := fun x => x }, { reft := fun _ => ⊤ }, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· refine
    { prov_mono := fun h => h
      reft_anti_mono := fun {_ _} _ => le_rfl
      top_le_reft_bot := le_rfl
      le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := fun {_ _} _ _ => htop _
      reft_le_prov_reft := fun {_} => le_rfl }
· intro x
  rfl
· intro x
  rfl
· intro h
  constructor
  · exact h
  · refine ⟨⟨⊤, ?_⟩⟩
    constructor <;> exact le_rfl

The APS fields are immediate: monotonicity for `P` is the given hypothesis, the `R`-fields collapse to reflexivity at `⊤`, and the mixed axiom follows from the assumed greatest-element property. op_000117.proof_sketch: Use the identity provability operator and the constant-top refutability operator. Register them with `letI` so the explicit `@ACR.APS α _ P R` witness elaborates against the intended local instances. The APS fields are immediate: `prov_mono` is identity monotonicity, the `reft` fields reduce to reflexivity at `⊤`, and the mixed axiom follows from the hypothesis `∀ x, x ≤ ⊤`. For the final implication, `ACR.Consistent α` is definitionally `¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥)`, and `⊤` is a Gödel fixpoint for the constant-top `R`.
op_000117.proof_text:
intro α _ htop
let P : ACR.Prov α := { prov := fun x => x }
let R : ACR.Reft α := { reft := fun _ => ⊤ }
letI : ACR.Prov α := P
letI : ACR.Reft α := R
have hA : @ACR.APS α _ P R := by
  refine
    { prov_mono := ?_
      reft_anti_mono := ?_
      top_le_reft_bot := ?_
      le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := ?_
      reft_le_prov_reft := ?_ }
  · intro x y hxy
    exact hxy
  · intro x y hxy
    exact le_rfl
  · exact le_rfl
  · intro x y hxy hyr
    exact htop x
  · intro x
    exact le_rfl
refine ⟨P, R, hA, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
· intro x
  rfl
· intro x
  rfl
· intro h
  constructor
  · exact h
  · refine ⟨⟨⊤, ?_⟩⟩
    constructor <;> exact le_rfl

op_000119.source_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ (_ : ACR α), ACR.Consistent α) ↔ Nontrivial α
op_000119.formalized_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ h : ACR α, @ACR.Consistent α h) ↔ Nontrivial α
op_000119.statement_formalization_notes: Made the `ACR` witness explicit as `h : ACR α` and applied `@ACR.Consistent α h` so the proposition does not rely on local typeclass inference inside the existential binder.

op_000119.proof_sketch: From a consistent `ACR` instance, `⊤` and `⊥` must be distinct: if `⊤ = ⊥`, reflexivity gives `⊤ ≤ ⊥`, contradicting consistency. Hence the carrier is nontrivial. Conversely, from `Nontrivial α`, pick `t ≠ b` and define an `ACR` structure with `x ≤ y` iff `x = y`, `⊤ = t`, and `⊥ = b`; then consistency is exactly `t ≠ b`.
op_000119.proof_text:
intro α
constructor
· rintro ⟨h, hc⟩
  letI : ACR α := h
  refine nontrivial_iff_exists_ne.mpr ⟨⊤, ⊥, ?_⟩
  intro hEq
  apply hc
  simpa [hEq] using (le_rfl : (⊤ : α) ≤ (⊤ : α))
· intro hnt
  rcases nontrivial_iff_exists_ne.mp hnt with ⟨t, b, htb⟩
  let h : ACR α :=
    { top := t
      bot := b
      le := fun x y => x = y
      le_refl := by
        intro x
        rfl
      le_trans := by
        intro x y z hxy hyz
        exact hxy.trans hyz }
  refine ⟨h, ?_⟩
  change ¬ ((t : α) = b)
  exact htb

op_000118.source_statement: For every cardinality `κ` with `κ ≠ 1`, there exists a type `α` of size `κ` carrying a consistent `ACR`/`Prov`/`Reft`/`APS`/`C5` structure such that `∀ x, x ≐ □x`, the order on `α` is total and antisymmetric, and `ACR.GödelFixpoint α` is nonempty.
op_000118.formalized_statement: ∀ κ : Cardinal, κ ≠ 1 → ∃ α : Type*, ∃ _ : ACR α, ∃ _ : ACR.Prov α, ∃ _ : ACR.Reft α, ∃ _ : ACR.APS α, ∃ _ : ACR.C5 α, ACR.Consistent α ∧ (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y ∨ y ≤ x) ∧ (∀ x y : α, x ≤ y → y ≤ x → x = y) ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ∧ Cardinal.lift (Cardinal.mk α) = κ
op_000118.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the carrier-size clause by replacing `# α` with `Cardinal.mk α`, keeping the original meaning and the conservative universe-lift comparison.

op_000118.proof_sketch: Instantiate the universal claim at κ = 0. The hypothesis `0 ≠ 1` is immediate. From the resulting witness `α` and the final size clause `Cardinal.lift (Cardinal.mk α) = 0`, simplify to `Cardinal.mk α = 0`, hence `IsEmpty α` by `Cardinal.mk_eq_zero_iff`. But an `ACR α` instance extends `Top α`, so `⊤ : α` exists, contradicting emptiness.
op_000118.proof_text:
by
  intro h
  rcases h 0 (by simp) with ⟨α, hACR, hProv, hReft, hAPS, hC5, hCons, hBox, hTot, hAnti, hGF, hκ⟩
  letI := hACR
  have hmk : Cardinal.mk α = 0 := by
    simpa using hκ
  have hEmpty : IsEmpty α := Cardinal.mk_eq_zero_iff.mp hmk
  exact hEmpty.false (⊤ : α)
op_000118.counterexample_text: Take `κ = 0`. If the statement were true, we would obtain some type `α` with an `ACR`/`Prov`/`Reft`/`APS`/`C5` package and `Cardinal.lift (Cardinal.mk α) = 0`. This forces `Cardinal.mk α = 0`, hence `α` is empty. But `ACR α` extends `Top α`, so there is a distinguished element `⊤ : α`, impossible for an empty type. Therefore the original universal statement is false.

op_000122.source_statement: Every nontrivial bounded linear order admits a canonical realization with `□x = x` and `⊠x = ⊤` that satisfies `ACR`, `Prov`, `Reft`, `APS`, and `C5`, is consistent, and has a Gödel fixpoint.
op_000122.formalized_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α], CanonicalRealization α
op_000122.statement_formalization_notes: Kept the prior meaning: the realization is an existential package of ACR, Prov, Reft, APS, and C5 structure on the same carrier, with □ acting as identity, ⊠ constantly top, consistency, and a Gödel fixpoint. Shortened the theorem face by moving the repeated witness bundle into a local abbrev.

op_000122.proof_sketch: Package the bounded linear order as an `ACR`, apply `thm_greatest_trivial_box_godel_000117` using `le_top`, define `C5` again by `le_top`, and discharge the consistency premise by showing `⊤ ≤ ⊥` would force `⊤ = ⊥`, contradicting `top_ne_bot`.
op_000122.prelude_code:
abbrev CanonicalRealization (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α] : Prop :=
  ∃ (hA : ACR α) (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA) (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA)
    (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR) (hC5 : @ACR.C5 α hA hP hR hAPS),
    (∀ x : α, @ACR.Prov.prov α hA hP x = x) ∧
    (∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α hA hR x = @Top.top α hA.toTop) ∧
    @ACR.Consistent α hA ∧ Nonempty (@ACR.GödelFixpoint α hA hR)
op_000122.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _
let hA : ACR α :=
  { top := ⊤
    bot := ⊥
    le := (· ≤ ·)
    le_refl := fun _ => le_rfl
    le_trans := fun _ _ _ hab hbc => le_trans hab hbc }
letI : ACR α := hA
have htop : ∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α) := fun x => le_top
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_greatest_trivial_box_godel_000117 α htop with
  ⟨hP, hR, hAPS, hbox, hreft, hgood⟩
letI : ACR.Prov α := hP
letI : ACR.Reft α := hR
letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS
have hC5 : @ACR.C5 α hA hP hR hAPS :=
  { le_top := fun {x} => le_top }
have hnot : ¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ (⊥ : α)) := by
  intro h
  exact top_ne_bot (le_antisymm h bot_le)
have hcg : ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := hgood hnot
exact ⟨hA, hP, hR, hAPS, hC5, hbox, hreft, hcg.1, hcg.2⟩

op_000122.proof_sketch: Keep the previous strategy, but build the `ACR` witness by reusing the existing `LinearOrder`/`BoundedOrder` infrastructure instead of trying to synthesize missing order fields manually. Then apply the derived existence theorem for the trivial box/top reft realization, package `C5` via `le_top`, and prove consistency from `top_ne_bot`.

abbrev CanonicalRealization (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α] : Prop :=
  ∃ (hA : ACR α) (hP : @ACR.Prov α hA) (hR : @ACR.Reft α hA)
    (hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR) (hC5 : @ACR.C5 α hA hP hR hAPS),
    (∀ x : α, @ACR.Prov.prov α hA hP x = x) ∧
    (∀ x : α, @ACR.Reft.reft α hA hR x = @Top.top α hA.toTop) ∧
    @ACR.Consistent α hA ∧ Nonempty (@ACR.GödelFixpoint α hA hR)
op_000122.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _
let hA : ACR α :=
  { toTop := inferInstance
    toBot := inferInstance
    toLE := inferInstance
    toPreorder := inferInstance }
letI : ACR α := hA
have htop : ∀ x : α, x ≤ (⊤ : α) := fun x => le_top
rcases AutomatedTheoryConstruction.thm_greatest_trivial_box_godel_000117 α htop with
  ⟨hP, hR, hAPS, hbox, hreft, hgood⟩
letI : ACR.Prov α := hP
letI : ACR.Reft α := hR
letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS
have hC5 : @ACR.C5 α hA hP hR hAPS :=
  { le_top := fun {x} => le_top }
have hnot : ¬ ((⊤ : α) ≤ (⊥ : α)) := by
  intro h
  exact top_ne_bot (le_antisymm h bot_le)
have hcg : ACR.Consistent α ∧ Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) := hgood hnot
exact ⟨hA, hP, hR, hAPS, hC5, hbox, hreft, hcg.1, hcg.2⟩

op_000124.source_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∀ h : ACR α, ¬ @ACR.Consistent α h) ↔ Subsingleton α
op_000124.formalized_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∀ h : ACR α, ¬ @ACR.Consistent α h) ↔ Subsingleton α
op_000124.statement_formalization_notes: The natural-language statement already matches valid Lean syntax in the repository's notation and uses explicit class arguments for `Consistent`.

op_000124.proof_sketch: Use `constructor`. For `→`, prove `Subsingleton α` via `subsingleton_iff`; if `a ≠ b`, then `α` is nontrivial, so equip `α` with an `ACR` structure whose order is equality and whose top/bot are `a`/`b`. In that instance, consistency is exactly `a ≠ b`, contradicting the hypothesis that every `ACR α` is inconsistent. For `←`, under `Subsingleton α`, any `ACR` instance has `⊤ = ⊥`, hence `(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥`, so consistency fails.
op_000124.proof_text:
classical
  intro α
  constructor
  · intro h
    rw [subsingleton_iff]
    intro a b
    by_contra hne
    have hns : ¬ Subsingleton α := by
      intro hs
      exact hne (Subsingleton.elim a b)
    letI : Nontrivial α := not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial.mp hns
    letI : ACR α where
      top := a
      bot := b
      le := Eq
      le_refl _ := rfl
      le_trans _ _ _ hab hbc := hab.trans hbc
    have hcons : @ACR.Consistent α inferInstance := by
      intro hab
      exact hne hab
    exact (h inferInstance) hcons
  · intro hs h
    letI : ACR α := h
    intro hcons
    exact hcons (by
      simpa [ACR.Inconsistent] using (le_of_eq (Subsingleton.elim (⊤ : α) (⊥ : α))))

op_000124.proof_sketch: For the forward direction, assume every `ACR α` is inconsistent. To show `Subsingleton α`, fix `a b : α` and argue by contradiction from `a ≠ b`. Define an `ACR` structure on `α` whose order is equality, whose top is `a`, and whose bottom is `b`; in that structure, inconsistency is exactly the equation `a = b`, so the structure is consistent, contradicting the hypothesis. For the reverse direction, assume `Subsingleton α`. For any `ACR` instance on `α`, `⊤ = ⊥` by subsingletonity, hence `(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥`, so consistency fails.
op_000124.proof_text:
classical
  intro α
  constructor
  · intro h
    rw [subsingleton_iff]
    intro a b
    by_contra hne
    have instLE : LE α := ⟨Eq⟩
    have instPreorder : Preorder α :=
      { toLE := instLE
        le_refl := by
          intro x
          rfl
        le_trans := by
          intro x y z hxy hyz
          exact hxy.trans hyz }
    letI : ACR α :=
      { toTop := ⟨a⟩
        toBot := ⟨b⟩
        toLE := instLE
        toPreorder := instPreorder }
    have hcons : @ACR.Consistent α inferInstance := by
      intro hab
      exact hne hab
    exact (h inferInstance) hcons
  · intro hs
    intro h
    letI : ACR α := h
    intro hcons
    apply hcons
    simpa [ACR.Inconsistent] using (le_of_eq (Subsingleton.elim (⊤ : α) (⊥ : α)))

op_000124.proof_sketch: For `→`, assume every `ACR α` is inconsistent. To prove `Subsingleton α`, use `subsingleton_iff` and argue by contradiction: if `a ≠ b`, put an `ACR` structure on `α` whose order is equality, top is `a`, and bottom is `b`. In that structure, inconsistency is exactly `a = b`, so it is consistent, contradicting the hypothesis. For `←`, if `α` is subsingleton, then in any `ACR` instance we have `⊤ = ⊥`, hence `(⊤ : α) ≤ ⊥`, so consistency fails.
op_000124.proof_text:
classical
intro α
constructor
· intro h
  rw [subsingleton_iff]
  intro a b
  by_contra hne
  letI : ACR α :=
    { top := a
      bot := b
      le := Eq
      lt := fun _ _ => False
      le_refl := by
        intro x
        rfl
      le_trans := by
        intro x y z hxy hyz
        exact hxy.trans hyz
      lt_iff_le_not_ge := by
        intro x y
        constructor
        · intro hlt
          cases hlt
        · rintro ⟨hxy, hyx⟩
          exact hyx hxy.symm }
  have hcons : @ACR.Consistent α inferInstance := by
    intro hab
    exact hne hab
  exact (h inferInstance) hcons
· intro hs
  intro h
  letI : ACR α := h
  intro hcons

op_000125.source_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ↔ Nonempty α
op_000125.formalized_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ↔ Nonempty α
op_000125.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already a valid Lean proposition using repository notation, so I preserved it verbatim.

op_000125.proof_sketch: Use `thm_acr_exists_iff_nonempty_000123` for the reverse implication by forgetting the extra structure. For `Nonempty α → ...`, choose `a : α` and equip `α` with the trivial preorder where every relation is `True`, with `⊤ = ⊥ = a`, `□x = x`, and `⊠x = x`; then all APS and C5 axioms are immediate, and `x ≐ □x` holds for every `x`.
op_000125.proof_text:
classical
intro α
constructor
· intro h
  rcases h with ⟨hA, _, _, _, _, _⟩
  exact (thm_acr_exists_iff_nonempty_000123 (α := α)).mp ⟨hA, trivial⟩
· intro h
  rcases h with ⟨a⟩
  let instA : ACR α :=
    { top := a
      bot := a
      le := fun _ _ => True
      le_refl := by
        intro _
        trivial
      le_trans := by
        intro _ _ _ _ _
        trivial }
  letI : ACR α := instA
  let instP : ACR.Prov α := { prov := fun x => x }
  letI : ACR.Prov α := instP
  let instR : ACR.Reft α := { reft := fun x => x }
  letI : ACR.Reft α := instR
  let instAPS : ACR.APS α :=
    { prov_mono := by
        intro _ _ _
        trivial
      reft_anti_mono := by
        intro _ _ _
        trivial
      top_le_reft_bot := by
        trivial
      le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
        intro _ _ _ _
        trivial
      reft_le_prov_reft := by
        intro _
        trivial }
  letI : ACR.APS α := instAPS
  let instC5 : ACR.C5 α :=
    { le_top := by
        intro _
        trivial }
  refine ⟨instA, instP, instR, instAPS, instC5, ?_⟩
  intro x
  constructor <;> trivial

op_000126.source_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ (_ : ACR α) (_ : ACR.Prov α) (_ : ACR.Reft α) (_ : ACR.APS α) (_ : ACR.C5 α), ACR.Consistent α ∧ ∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) ↔ Nontrivial α
op_000126.formalized_statement: ∀ α : Type*, (∃ hA : ACR α, ∃ hP : @ACR.Prov α hA, ∃ hR : @ACR.Reft α hA, ∃ hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR, ∃ hC5 : @ACR.C5 α hA hP hR hAPS, @ACR.Consistent α hA ∧ ∀ x : α, @ACR.Equivalent α hA x (@ACR.Prov.prov α hA hP x)) ↔ Nontrivial α
op_000126.statement_formalization_notes: Used fully explicit class parameters because the existentially introduced structures are not available as local instances for notation-based elaboration.

op_000126.proof_sketch: Forward direction follows immediately from `thm_consistent_acr_iff_nontrivial_000119` by forgetting the extra structure. For the reverse direction, from `Nontrivial α` choose distinct `bot0` and `top0`, build the three-valued preorder `x ≤ y := x = bot0 ∨ y = top0 ∨ x = y`, take `prov := id`, `reft := fun _ => top0`, verify `APS` and `C5` directly, then consistency is exactly `top0 ≠ bot0` and `x ≐ □x` is reflexivity.
op_000126.proof_text:
intro α
constructor
· rintro ⟨hA, hP, hR, hAPS, hC5, hCons, hEq⟩
  exact (thm_consistent_acr_iff_nontrivial_000119 α).mp ⟨hA, hCons⟩
· intro hN
  classical
  letI : Nontrivial α := hN
  obtain ⟨bot0, top0, hne⟩ := exists_pair_ne α
  let hA : ACR α :=
    { top := top0
      bot := bot0
      le := fun x y => x = bot0 ∨ y = top0 ∨ x = y
      le_refl := by
        intro x
        exact Or.inr (Or.inr rfl)
      le_trans := by
        intro a b c hab hbc
        rcases hab with ha | hb | hab
        · exact Or.inl ha
        · rcases hbc with hb' | hc | hbc'
          · exfalso
            exact hne (hb'.symm.trans hb)
          · exact Or.inr (Or.inl hc)
          · exact Or.inr (Or.inl (hbc'.symm ▸ hb))
        · simpa [hab] using hbc }
  let hP : @ACR.Prov α hA := { prov := fun x => x }
  let hR : @ACR.Reft α hA := { reft := fun _ => top0 }
  let hAPS : @ACR.APS α hA hP hR :=
    { prov_mono := by
        intro x y hxy
        simpa using hxy
      reft_anti_mono := by
        intro x y hxy
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl)
      top_le_reft_bot := by
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl)
      le_reft_top_of_le_prov_of_le_reft := by
        intro x y hxy hxr
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl)
      reft_le_prov_reft := by
        intro x
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl) }
  let hC5 : @ACR.C5 α hA hP hR hAPS :=
    { le_top := by
        intro x
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl rfl) }
  letI : ACR α := hA
  letI : ACR.Prov α := hP
  letI : ACR.Reft α := hR
  letI : ACR.APS α := hAPS
  letI : ACR.C5 α := hC5
  refine ⟨hA, hP, hR, hAPS, hC5, ?_⟩
  constructor
  · intro hIncon
    rcases hIncon with htb | hbt | htb
    · exact hne htb.symm
    · exact hne hbt
    · exact hne htb.symm
  · intro x
    constructor <;> exact Or.inr (Or.inr rfl)

op_000128.source_statement: For every type `α`, any `ACR + Prov + Reft + APS + C5` realization on `α` with `∀ x : α, x ≐ □x` satisfies `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ Nonempty α`, and in the canonical trivial-`□` regime every element of `α` is a Henkin fixed point.
op_000128.formalized_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → ((Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) ↔ Nonempty α) ∧ ∀ x : α, ∃ h : ACR.HenkinFixpoint α, h.1 = x)
op_000128.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the second clause as every element being realized by some Henkin fixed point with that carrier value.

op_000128.proof_sketch: Introduce the universal fixed-point hypothesis `hbox`. For `Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α) → Nonempty α`, project a Henkin fixed point to its carrier. For the converse, any `x : α` gives the Henkin fixed point `⟨x, hbox x⟩`. The realization clause uses the same witness for each `x`, with carrier equality by reflexivity.
op_000128.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ hbox
constructor
· constructor
  · intro h
    rcases h with ⟨h⟩
    exact ⟨h.1⟩
  · intro h
    rcases h with ⟨x⟩
    exact ⟨⟨x, hbox x⟩⟩
· intro x
  exact ⟨⟨x, hbox x⟩, rfl⟩

op_000127.source_statement: For every type `α`, any consistent `ACR + Prov + Reft + APS + C5` realization on `α` with `∀ x : α, x ≐ □x` and `∀ x : α, ⊠x ≐ ⊤` satisfies `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≐ ⊤`, and moreover every Gödel fixed point is equivalent to `⊤`.
op_000127.formalized_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], ACR.Consistent α → (∀ x : α, x ≐ □x) → (∀ x : α, ⊠x ≐ (⊤ : α)) → ((Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ ∃ x : α, x ≐ (⊤ : α)) ∧ ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ (⊤ : α))
op_000127.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the statement as a universally quantified implication over any `ACR + Prov + Reft + APS + C5` realization, bundling the biconditional and the universal characterization of Gödel fixed points into a conjunction.

op_000127.proof_sketch: Use `ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top` and the hypothesis `∀ x, ⊠x ≐ ⊤` at `x = ⊤` to show every Gödel fixpoint is equivalent to `⊤`. Then `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → ∃ x, x ≐ ⊤` is witnessed by the carrier of any fixpoint, and conversely from `x ≐ ⊤` together with `⊠x ≐ ⊤` we get `x ≐ ⊠x`, yielding a Gödel fixpoint.
op_000127.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hReftTop
have hgf_top : ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ (⊤ : α) := by
  intro g
  have hg_reft_top : g.1 ≐ ⊠(⊤ : α) := ACR.gf_equiv_reft_top (g := g)
  have hreft_top : ⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ (⊤ : α) := hReftTop (⊤ : α)
  exact ⟨le_trans hg_reft_top.1 hreft_top.1, le_trans hreft_top.2 hg_reft_top.2⟩
constructor
· constructor
  · intro h
    obtain ⟨g⟩ := h
    exact ⟨g.1, hgf_top g⟩
  · intro h
    obtain ⟨x, hx⟩ := h
    refine ⟨⟨x, ?_⟩⟩
    have hreft_x : ⊠x ≐ (⊤ : α) := hReftTop x
    exact ⟨le_trans hx.1 hreft_x.2, le_trans hreft_x.1 hx.2⟩
· exact hgf_top

op_000129.source_statement: ∀ (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α], letI := Classical.choose (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α)); ∀ g : ACR.GödelFixpoint α, g.1 ≐ (⊤ : α)
op_000129.statement_formalization_notes: Kept the previous explicit chosen-instance encoding. The source statement's `letI := Classical.choose ...` is not suitable inside the proposition and does not by itself expose the needed `Reft` instance for `ACR.GödelFixpoint`, so the formalization conservatively uses small noncomputable abbreviations for the chosen `ACR` and `Reft` witnesses and states the equivalence with explicit instance arguments.

op_000129.proof_sketch: Extract the canonical realization witness produced by `thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122`. Its stored `hreft` component says the chosen canonical refutation sends every element to `⊤`. Rewriting the Gödel fixpoint equation `g.2 : g.1 ≐ ⊠g.1` with that fact gives exactly `g.1 ≐ ⊤`.
op_000129.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ g
have hreft :
    ∀ x : α,
      @ACR.Reft.reft α (chosen_canonical_acr α) (chosen_canonical_reft α) x =
        @Top.top α (chosen_canonical_acr α).toTop :=
  (Classical.choose_spec
    (Classical.choose_spec
      (Classical.choose_spec
        (Classical.choose_spec
          (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α)))))).2.1
simpa [ACR.Equivalent, hreft g.1] using g.2

op_000129.proof_sketch: The previous argument was fine, but the scratch file was missing the explicit canonical instance names used by the fixed statement. Define `chosen_canonical_acr` and `chosen_canonical_reft` by unpacking `thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122` with `Classical.choose`, then reuse the canonical `hreft` fact to rewrite the Gödel fixpoint equation `g.2 : g.1 ≐ ⊠g.1` into `g.1 ≐ ⊤`.
op_000129.prelude_code:
noncomputable abbrev chosen_canonical_acr (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α] : ACR α :=
  Classical.choose (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α))

noncomputable abbrev chosen_canonical_reft (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α] :
    @ACR.Reft α (chosen_canonical_acr α) :=
  Classical.choose
    (Classical.choose_spec (Classical.choose_spec (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α))))
op_000129.proof_text:
intro α _ _ _ g
have hreft :
    ∀ x : α,
      @ACR.Reft.reft α (chosen_canonical_acr α) (chosen_canonical_reft α) x =
        @Top.top α (chosen_canonical_acr α).toTop :=
  (Classical.choose_spec
    (Classical.choose_spec
      (Classical.choose_spec
        (Classical.choose_spec
          (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α)))))).2.1
simpa [ACR.Equivalent, hreft g.1] using g.2

op_000129.proof_sketch: The previous proof was selecting the wrong component from the canonical-realization witness: four `Classical.choose_spec` projections only reach the stage before the final conjunction, where the first available theorem is the box identity `∀ x, □x = x`. One more `choose_spec` reaches the conjunction containing both `hbox` and `hreft`, and then `.2.1` is exactly the refutation equation `∀ x, ⊠x = ⊤`. Rewriting the Gödel-fixpoint equation `g.2 : g.1 ≐ ⊠g.1` with that equality yields the goal.
op_000129.prelude_code:
noncomputable abbrev chosen_canonical_acr (α : Type*) [LinearOrder α] [BoundedOrder α] [Nontrivial α] : ACR α :=
  Classical.choose (thm_bounded_linear_canonical_realization_000122 (α := α))

simpa [ACR.Equivalent, hreft g.1] using g.2
